The Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon is a masterpiece of French colonial architecture. Even with the ongoing renovation, its timeless charm and grandeur are still very much intact. The vibrant red brick facade, imported from France, remains a striking feature of the city's skyline.
It's a quintessential spot for both locals and tourists to enjoy the peaceful atmosphere right in the heart of District 1. I highly recommend visiting in the evening when the lighting makes the entire structure look absolutely breathtaking. It’s a perfect blend of historical significance and urban beauty.

Although it was still under renovation as of December 2025, the cathedral looks really striking at night with its outline lighting illuminating the entire structure. You can get a top-down view by heading to Diamond Plaza and taking the escalator or elevator up to the top floor, where there’s an open viewing area. Just a heads-up that it does get pretty crowded with people taking photos, so you’ll have to find a spot and work with it lol.

The Notre-Dame Cathedral in Saigon (Ho Chi Minh City) was built between 1863 and 1880 by the French. It was intended to serve the Catholic community living there and to show French culture and influence. Many of the bricks, stones, and stained-glass windows were imported directly from France.
With its twin towers and red brick façade, the cathedral quickly became a landmark of the city. Despite wars and political changes, it has remained standing and is still used for church services today.
Today, Notre-Dame Cathedral in Saigon is both a reminder of the colonial past and a lasting symbol within modern Ho Chi Minh City.

This magnificent symbol of Saigon and of Vietnam as a whole is an extraordinary and truly unique cathedral, built with materials originally produced in Europe. Although it has unfortunately been undergoing restoration for quite a long time, it is clearly visible that the work is progressing steadily and with great care. With every section freed from scaffolding, the cathedral reveals itself as even more beautiful, more refined, and more impressive than before. Particularly admirable is the fact that the materials prepared for the restoration have also been manufactured in Europe, ensuring full authenticity and deep respect for the original architectural vision.
One can only hope that the restoration and conservation works will be completed soon, so that this remarkable landmark can once again shine in its full glory. The cathedral is an incredibly attractive place, not only because of its architecture but also thanks to the wonderful square on which it stands. The square is vibrant and full of life, populated by many young people, charming cafés, restaurants, and an unmistakable atmosphere that blends energy, elegance, and cultural depth.
The cathedral is especially magical around the New Year period, when it is illuminated with hundreds of thousands of lights, creating a truly breathtaking and almost fairy-tale-like scene. The star placed in front of the cathedral, constructed entirely from illuminated elements, is exceptionally original and visually striking. Equally impressive is the fully restored statue of the Madonna and Child standing before the cathedral, which represents an important and integral part of the great artistic heritage of Vietnam’s largest city.
This place is absolutely recommended for every visitor passing through Saigon. And once the restoration is fully completed and the interior becomes accessible again, the experience will be even richer and more profound. Visitors will be able to admire the beautiful works of art preserved inside the cathedral and fully appreciate its spiritual and artistic significance. For Christians in particular, this visit carries an added sense of joy, meaning, and inner peace.
A truly unique place that deserves to be visited without hesitation — not merely as a tourist attraction, but as a living symbol of history, faith, art, and culture, deeply woven into the soul of Saigon.

Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon is a majestic French-built landmark in District 1. Its red-brick twin spires, stained glass windows, and ornate interior convey serenity amid bustling downtown. Currently under thorough restoration (active since 2017, now expected to finish by 2027), work includes roof repairs, tower stone replacement, cross restoration, new ventilation, and a future pipe organ. Renovation is careful and respectful — Masses continue, though scaffolding and fences limit access. Expect ambiance, history, and still-unfinished charm.

If you're visiting Ho Chi Minh City, the Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica is an unmissable landmark. Located right in the heart of District 1, this iconic church was built in the late 19th century by the French and stands as a stunning example of Romanesque and Gothic architecture.

What caught my attention was the cathedral’s twin bell towers, red-tiled roof, and the original brick facade that has withstood time remarkably well. The building exudes a peaceful, timeless charm amidst the hustle and bustle of the city. Although the interior is currently undergoing renovation, you can still feel the sacred and serene atmosphere inside.

One of the great things about this spot is the surrounding area—it's full of cafes, small parks, and pedestrian-friendly streets, perfect for strolling, relaxing, and people-watching. The cathedral is not only a place of worship but also a powerful symbol of the city's cultural and historical heritage.

Tip: Visit in the early morning or around sunset for the best light and fewer crowds—ideal for beautiful photos and a more tranquil experience.

Visiting the Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon was like stepping into a piece of history right in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City. 🏰

The architecture immediately caught my eye – the striking red bricks imported from France and the twin bell towers standing tall against the bustling modern city backdrop. It’s a perfect mix of old-world charm and vibrant city life.

As I walked inside (though parts of the interior were restricted due to restoration work), the tranquil atmosphere felt like a retreat from the city’s chaos. The stained-glass windows were beautiful, casting colorful patterns across the walls, and the craftsmanship on display was truly stunning.

The outside courtyard is equally picturesque, especially the Virgin Mary statue in front of the cathedral. It’s a peaceful spot where locals and tourists gather – some to pray, others to just take in the beauty and serenity of the space.

What stood out to me was how the cathedral feels like more than just a religious site – it’s a cultural symbol that reflects Ho Chi Minh’s colonial past and its connection to French history.

Quick Tips:-
- Visit early morning or late afternoon to avoid crowds and enjoy the cooler weather.
- Combine your trip with a visit to the nearby Saigon Central Post Office, just a short walk away.
- If you’re into photography, this place is a dream – every angle is Instagram-worthy! 📸

Overall, Notre Dame Cathedral is a must-visit if you're in Ho Chi Minh City, whether you're interested in history, architecture, or just looking for a peaceful spot to pause and reflect. ❤️

Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon is one of the most iconic landmarks in Ho Chi Minh City, and a must-visit for any traveler. Built between 1877 and 1880 by French colonists, its stunning neo-Romanesque architecture, red brick façade, and twin bell towers make it a striking sight in the heart of the city. Even though the interior is currently under renovation (as of 2025), the exterior alone is worth the visit.

The square in front of the cathedral is a popular gathering spot, with a statue of the Virgin Mary adding to the peaceful atmosphere. It’s surrounded by beautiful gardens and is a perfect place for photos, especially in the morning when the light is soft.

Located right across from the Saigon Central Post Office, it’s easy to combine both visits in one trip. The area is lively yet safe, with plenty of cafes and shops nearby to rest and enjoy a drink.

Tip: Visit early in the day to avoid the crowds and heat, and take time to walk around to appreciate the details of the building. A beautiful blend of history, architecture, and culture that captures the charm of old Saigon.

The Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon is a breathtaking landmark that captivates visitors with its striking architecture and rich history. The cathedral's majestic façade, built with red bricks and adorned with stunning stained glass, stands as a testament to the city’s colonial past.

What truly enhances its beauty, especially in the evening, is the enchanting display of small LED lights that illuminate the structure. These lights create a magical atmosphere, making the cathedral appear as if it is adorned with twinkling stars against the night sky. The effect is nothing short of mesmerizing, allowing the intricate details of the building to shine even more brightly.

As you stand in front of the cathedral, the serene ambiance invites you to take a moment to appreciate the artistry and craftsmanship that went into its construction. The surrounding gardens provide a lovely space to relax and soak in the beauty of the scene. Whether you're a history enthusiast, an architecture lover, or simply looking for a picturesque spot, the Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon is an absolute must-visit. Don’t forget your camera—you’ll want to capture the magic of this iconic landmark!

Situated right in the heart of Hochiminh City is the most famous landmark as well as core Cathedral of the city, Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral (translated as “Nhà thờ Đức Bà Sài Gòn).
Notre-Dame Cathedral, or Notre-Dame Basilica to be exact, is a famous age-long cathedral in Southern Vietnam. It is a magnificient building located at the paris Square in Hochiminh City downtown, attracting not only Catholics but also most tourists for its neo-Romanesque style architecture and a sacred atmosphere.
Historical story
Established by French colonists, the cathedral was constructed between 1863 and 1880. It has two bell towers, reaching a height of over 58 meters (190 feet). Following the French conquest of Cochinchina and Saigon, the Roman Catholic Church established a community and religious services for French colonialists.
The first church was built on Rue 5 (today’s Ngo Duc Ke Street). This had been a Vietnamese pagoda, which had been abandoned during the war. Bishop Lefevre decided to make this pagoda a church. In 1959, Bishop Joseph Pham Van Thien, whose jurisdiction included Saigon parish, attended Holy Mother Congress held in Vatican and ordered a Peaceful Notre Dame statue made with granite in Rome. When the statue arrived in Saigon, on February 16th, 1959, Bishop Pham Van Thien held a ceremony to install the statue on the empty base and presented the title of “Regina Pacis”. It was Bishop Pham Van Thien who wrote the prayers “Notre-Dame bless the peace to Vietnam”. On the following day, Cardinal Aganianian came from Rome to chair the closing ceremony of the Holy Mother Congress and solemnly chaired the ceremony for the statue, thus the cathedral was then-on called Notre-Dame Cathedral.
Honored Basilica Status
The cathedral boasts its honored status as a Basilica consecrated tens of years ago. In 1960, the Vatican founded a Roman Catholic dioceses in Vietnam and assigned archbishops to Hanoi, Huế and Saigon. Notre-Dame cathedral was titled Saigon Chief Cathedral. Yet, in 1962, Vatican anointed the Saigon Chief Cathedral, conferred it basilique in honor of the tri-cenetary of the Bishopric of Saigon. Since then, this cathedral was called Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica, which has enhanced its value and absorbed hundreds of thousands of catholics as well as international tourists to pay a visit.
The special architectural style
Apart from the religious meaning that attracts tourists, the even more special captivating point of Notre-Dame Cathedral is its special neo-Romanesque style of architecture. During its construction, all its red bricks were imported from Marseilles and colored glass windows made in France’s Chartres Province, France. Tiles have been carved with the words Guichard Carvin, Marseille St André France (perhaps stating the locality where the tiles were produced). Some tiles were carved with the words “Wang-Tai Saigon.” Many tiles have since been made in Saigon to replace the broken tiles caused by war. There are 56 glass squares supplied by the Lorin firm of Chartres province in France. The cathedral foundation was designed to bear 10 times the weight of the cathedral. In front of the cathedral is a statue of the Virgin Mary. On a whole, the neo-Romanesque architecture and two 58m-high square towers tipped with iron spires dominate the city’s sky line, creating a beautiful religious site!
Weekly Service Schedule
Full services in both Vietnamese and English are held every Sunday morning and are well attended by Vietnamese and foreigners alike. Other services are held throughout the week. Hence, visitors who wish to attend mass should go on Sunday at 9.30a.m.
Must see place in Saigon

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral is one of the most prominent and iconic landmarks in Ho Chi Minh City. Even after many visits, standing before the cathedral still evokes a special feeling – a sense of solemnity and peace amidst the bustling city.

The first impression is its ancient architecture with two towering bell towers. The entire structure is built of red brick, reflecting a very distinctive European style. The bricks have retained their beautiful color over time, creating a sense of durability and history. The spacious and airy grounds in front are a popular spot for people to stop, take photos, rest, or simply admire the view.

The area surrounding the cathedral is quite tranquil compared to the outside world. Upon entering the cathedral grounds, one naturally feels a sense of calm, free from the hurried pace. Whether religious or not, everyone who comes here feels the solemnity and serenity.

Notre Dame Cathedral is not only a religious site but also a famous tourist destination, closely linked to the history and development of Saigon. Its convenient location in the heart of the city, near the City Post Office and Nguyen Van Binh book street, makes it easy to combine visits to multiple attractions.

Early mornings or late afternoons are the best times to visit. Mornings are cool and peaceful. In the evenings, when the city lights up, the surroundings of the cathedral are beautiful, a blend of ancient and modern, making for stunning photos.

In my personal opinion, Notre Dame Cathedral is suitable for:

• People attending religious services and praying
• Tourists interested in architecture and history
• Those seeking a moment of peace in the city center
• Couples and friends taking souvenir photos

Overall, it's a very worthwhile place to visit when in Ho Chi Minh City. You don't need a lot of time; a short stop is enough to appreciate the beauty and value of this landmark. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ 5-star rating – a historical landmark, beautiful architecture, and a dignified and peaceful atmosphere.

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral is one of the iconic architectural landmarks of Ho Chi Minh City.

• Construction: 1877–1880, during the French colonial period

• Architecture: Roman-Gothic style; materials (brick, stained glass) mainly imported from France

• Notable features: Two bell towers approximately 58 meters high, unplastered red brick facade; interior features numerous stained glass windows

• Significance: An important center of Catholic activity, a historical and cultural symbol of the city.

The cathedral is often mentioned as a prominent tourist attraction associated with the history and development of Saigon – Ho Chi Minh City. Restoration work began on July 1, 2017 and has lasted for many years, with completion expected in 2027.

Notre Dame Cathedral: The Ancient "Red Heart" Amidst the Flow of Time

In the minds of Saigonese people and visitors from afar, Notre Dame Cathedral is not just a religious building, but has become an enduring symbol, the "representative face" of the city's architectural beauty and history for over a century. Located right in the heart of District 1, where bustling streets intersect, this cathedral stands as a silent, majestic, and nostalgic witness.

The most striking feature of Notre Dame Cathedral is its exquisite and unique Romanesque architecture. The entire structure is built with crimson red bricks imported directly from Marseille (France). Remarkably, after hundreds of years exposed to the harsh southern sun and rain, the bricks retain their vibrant, fresh pink color, untouched by moss and lichen, unlike other ancient structures. Two towering bell towers reaching towards the sky, along with the serene statue of Our Lady of Peace standing in the center of the square, create a majestic and magnificent yet approachable and benevolent overall effect. This is the "legendary" backdrop that anyone visiting Saigon wants to capture in a commemorative photograph.

Beyond its physical beauty, Notre Dame Cathedral is also the soul of Saigon's "sidewalk culture." The park area surrounding the cathedral is the cradle of the "sidewalk coffee culture." In the early morning or late afternoon, the feeling of sitting on the sidewalk, holding a plastic cup of coffee, watching the pigeons fly around, and listening to the church bells ringing is an indescribable poetic experience. That's when Saigon appears most peaceful and romantic, completely separate from the hustle and bustle of car horns and dust.

However, if you visit Notre Dame Cathedral at this time, you will encounter a major drawback: the ongoing restoration work. Since 2017, the church has undergone the largest restoration in its history. As a result, the area surrounding the building is now frequently fenced off with metal scaffolding, obscuring some of the original architectural beauty (especially the rear area and the two bell towers). More importantly, access to the interior is now very limited. Previously, visitors could admire the magnificent interior with its soaring vaulted ceilings and vibrant stained-glass windows, but now, to ensure construction safety and maintain the church's sanctity, it is often closed to the general public, only opening to a limited number of parishioners for services at fixed times. This may be disappointing for those wishing to explore the "soul" of the sanctuary.

Furthermore, because it's located right at a roundabout with no traffic lights, crossing the road to reach the statue of the Virgin Mary or coming from the Post Office requires extreme caution, especially for tourists unfamiliar with Vietnam's unique traffic system.

In short, despite being covered in scaffolding during the restoration project, Notre Dame Cathedral retains its powerful allure. It's a masterpiece of brick and stone, a place that preserves faith and serves as a spiritual anchor for the city's people. Even without being able to fully admire it inside, simply standing outside and gazing at the vibrant red bricks against the blue sky, one can still fully feel the pulse of a Saigon that is both ancient and majestic.

A small note for you:

For photography: The best vantage point currently is from the 30/4 Park or slightly towards Dong Khoi Street to capture the facade and the statue of the Virgin Mary, avoiding most of the scaffolding behind.

Experience the "coffee on the sidewalk": Try buying a cup of coffee from a street vendor, find a shady spot under a nearby old tree, and sit and admire the church; you'll find it very enjoyable that time seems to slow down.

Ceremony: If you are Catholic and wish to attend Mass, please check the latest Mass times on the Archdiocese's website, as times may change due to construction.

⭐ Overview

Notre Dame Cathedral in Saigon is a prominent architectural landmark in the heart of District 1, Ho Chi Minh City – possessing historical and cultural value and a very popular tourist attraction.

Its architecture is in the Roman and Gothic style, with two bell towers approximately 57 meters high.

Its convenient location (close to many other attractions) makes it ideal for photography, strolling, and combining with other activities.

⸻

✅ Advantages

• Impressive architecture: Red bricks imported from France, the roof, bell towers, and details clearly reflect the French colonial period.

• Spiritual & cultural significance: A place of worship for the Catholic community, and also a tourist attraction for everyone – both solemn and open to visitors.

• Beautiful scenery: Located on a large square, it's easy to stroll and take photos, especially in the morning or at sunset.

• Free entry (for exterior visits only) — suitable for all locals and tourists.

⸻

⚠️ Disadvantages & Notes
• Currently undergoing restoration/maintenance, so some areas may have restricted access or be under construction.

• Due to its popularity, it will be crowded with tourists and photographers during peak hours — if you want a quieter space, visit early in the morning or on weekdays.

• Located near the city center, parking and transportation may be somewhat complicated, and you may need to plan for a longer stay.

• Inside the mosque — maintain a respectful attitude and dress appropriately if entering for worship or visiting this sacred area.

⸻

🎯 Suggestions for Visiting
• Best visiting times: around 8 AM or 4-6 PM for good light and to avoid harsh sunlight.

• Bring a camera or phone with good photo quality — the architecture and lighting are beautiful, making for great photo opportunities.

• If you want to enter the cathedral for a service or a moment of reflection, go during service hours (check the schedule beforehand) to experience the right atmosphere.

• Dress neatly and respectfully when visiting the interior.

• Combine your visit: after visiting the cathedral, you can stop by the square in front, or walk to nearby attractions such as the Ho Chi Minh City Post Office, the Independence Palace, etc., for a more complete experience.

The Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon (often called the "Red Church," and commonly known in English as the Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon) is located in the Paris Commune Square area of ​​District 1, Ho Chi Minh City, next to the Saigon Central Post Office. It is a classic French colonial architectural landmark in the city center.

The church was primarily built during the French colonial period, with the construction of the existing main building commonly recorded as occurring between 1877 and 1880.

After the placement of the statue of Our Lady in the forecourt in 1959, the name "Notre-Dame" became more widely used. The church has been undergoing large-scale restoration work in recent years, and many reports and tourist information mention that the project has been extended, with completion expected around 2027.

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral – the Basilica of Our Lady – is the most famous architectural symbol and considered the "heart" of Ho Chi Minh City. Notre Dame Cathedral is not only a religious building but also an invaluable cultural and historical heritage.

It is a masterpiece perfectly combining Roman and Gothic styles, exuding majesty and antiquity. The most unique feature that leaves a lasting impression on visitors is the exposed red Marseille brickwork, unplastered yet retaining its vibrant pink color and remaining free from moss and mildew despite nearly 150 years of weathering. The two towering bell towers create a powerful focal point on the city center's skyline.

However, it should be noted that the cathedral is currently undergoing a large-scale and prolonged restoration. The presence of scaffolding surrounding it may somewhat restrict the view and the ability to take panoramic photos. Nevertheless, this demonstrates the city and the Church's immense respect and efforts in preserving this heritage for generations to come.
Besides its architectural beauty, the area around the cathedral, with the Paris Commune Park, the statue of Our Lady of Peace, and the friendly pigeons, creates a distinctive cultural space. Sitting and enjoying a morning coffee while gazing at the ancient cathedral amidst the modern traffic is a very Saigon experience, both nostalgic and vibrant.
Despite its ongoing healing process, Notre Dame Cathedral remains an irreplaceable destination, the soul and pride of the people of Ho Chi Minh City.

Located right in the heart of District 1 of Ho Chi Minh City, Notre Dame Cathedral is an architectural masterpiece, combining Roman and Gothic styles, and nearly 150 years old. The building has become a unique cultural and religious symbol, attracting numerous tourists.

Notre Dame Cathedral, also officially known as the Immaculate Conception Cathedral Basilica (English: Immaculate Conception Cathedral Basilica, French: Cathédrale Notre-Dame de Saïgon, or simply Notre Dame Cathedral), is the cathedral of the Archdiocese of Ho Chi Minh City.

Notre Dame Cathedral is considered one of the most famous architectural masterpieces of Saigon, built during the resistance against the French and serving as a historical witness to the formation and development of Ho Chi Minh City.

Since its construction on October 7, 1877, Notre Dame Cathedral has undergone three restorations:

The first restoration involved adding a zinc roof to the two bell towers in 1895.

The second, in 1903, involved renovating the cathedral's facade, adding a flower garden, and erecting a statue of St. Paul.

The third was the erection of the statue of Our Lady of Peace in 1959.

And currently: The restoration of Notre Dame Cathedral began in 2017 and is expected to be completed in 2027, but due to difficulties in importing materials from abroad, the prolonged Covid-19 pandemic has made importing repair materials from France and Germany difficult and limited. The restoration project is expected to be completed by 2027.

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral (Vietnamese: Nhà thờ Đức Bà Sài Gòn) is one of the most famous landmarks in Ho Chi Minh City (formerly Saigon).

Located in the city center of District 1, it is an important historical, architectural, and religious site.

Here's a detailed introduction:

Historical Background

• Construction Period: 1863-1880, built during the French colonial period.

• Architectural Style: Romanesque Revival style with Gothic elements.

• Designer: Designed by French architect Jules Bourard.

• Building Materials: Almost all the building materials, including the red bricks and stained glass windows, were imported from France.

Architectural Features

• Twin Bell Towers: The church's two bell towers, reaching 58 meters in height and topped with iron spires, are iconic structures on the city skyline.

• Interior Design: The church's interior is simple yet elegant, featuring stained-glass windows, wooden pews, and a central altar.

• Statue of the Virgin Mary: A marble statue of the Virgin Mary, known as the "Mother of Peace," stands in the square in front of the church.

It is said that the statue "weeped" in 2005, attracting many pilgrims.

Significance

• Religious Center: This church is the cathedral of the Catholic Archdiocese of Ho Chi Minh City and remains an active place of worship.

• Tourist Attraction: As a popular tourist attraction, the church draws many visitors, often visited in conjunction with nearby landmarks such as the Saigon Central Post Office.

• Restoration Work: Since 2017, the church has been undergoing restoration work. Currently, the interior is temporarily closed to the public, but visitors can still admire the exterior and take photos. Address: 01 Công xã Paris, Bến Nghé, Quận 1, Hồ Chí Minh 70000

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral, a masterpiece of Romanesque and Gothic architecture, is not only a symbol of faith for Catholic parishioners but also a captivating destination for tourists from around the world visiting Ho Chi Minh City. Designed by architect Jules Bourard, the cathedral was officially completed and consecrated in 1880 after three years of construction, at a total cost of approximately 2.5 million French francs at the time.

The major restoration project of Notre Dame Cathedral began on July 1, 2017, and is being undertaken by Monument Group (Belgium).

Father Ho Van Xuan stated that Notre Dame Cathedral is a historical monument and a landmark of Ho Chi Minh City, therefore the restoration must be done to the best of its ability. Notre Dame Cathedral in Saigon is the first ancient church in Asia to be restored by European experts.

Notre Dame Cathedral, or more fully the Basilica of the Immaculate Conception, is the cathedral of the Archdiocese of Ho Chi Minh City and one of the four basilicas in Vietnam. The cathedral was built in 1877 and completed in 1880 during the French colonial period, making it over 140 years old. The architect was Jules Bourard. When first completed, the cathedral had only two square bell towers. At the end of 1894, metal spires were installed on the two towers, making the cathedral taller and more prominent from afar. The two towers house six bronze bells weighing a total of nearly 29 tons. The sound of the bells can be heard from over 10 km away. The bricks used in construction are exposed brick, not plastered. This type of brick has the characteristic of resisting moss and mold growth. The building materials were all imported from France, such as Marseille tiles and Toulouse bricks. Having weathered many historical ups and downs, the church is currently undergoing renovations due to its dilapidated condition, with completion expected in 2027.

Notre Dame Cathedral is the shortened name for the Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral, officially known as the Basilica of the Immaculate Conception. It is located at Paris Commune Square, Ben Nghe Ward, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City.

With a history spanning over 140 years, the cathedral is one of the most prominent religious architectural works in the heart of Saigon. Construction of Notre Dame Cathedral began in 1877 and was completed in 1880, designed and supervised by architect J. Bourad.

On April 11, 1880, the dedication and inauguration ceremony of the cathedral in District 1 was organized by Father Colombert, with the presence of the Governor of Cochinchina, Le Myre de Vilers. Because all construction and interior decoration costs for this building were covered by the Governor-General's Office of Cochinchina, totaling 2,500,000 French francs at the time, the building was initially called the State Church - built and managed by the French State.
Notre Dame Cathedral offers a warm and inviting atmosphere, attracting tourists every day. If you're lucky, you might even get to hear the church bells ringing in the tower.

Located in the heart of the bustling city, Notre-Dame Cathedral stands out as an architectural and spiritual symbol of the city's people. Built between 1877 and 1880, this structure is a living testament to the harmonious blend of history, religion, and European art in the heart of Indochina.

The entire cathedral is constructed from distinctive red bricks imported from Marseille (France), retaining their original color and durability after more than 140 years. Two towering bell towers reach towards the Saigon skyline, along with the statue of Our Lady of Peace in the center of the square, creating a familiar image for both locals and tourists. Every morning and evening, you can see hundreds of people stopping in front of the cathedral – some praying, some taking photos, others simply sitting quietly watching the traffic go by.

The interior space, though not overly large, is incredibly refined – light from the stained-glass windows illuminates the wooden pews, creating an atmosphere that is both sacred and peaceful. When the bells chime amidst the bustling city, the feeling is indescribable – a moment of serene tranquility in the midst of the hectic pace of life.

Surrounding the cathedral are other famous attractions such as the Central Post Office, the Book Street, and the April 30th Park, forming a distinctive architectural complex that every visitor to Saigon should see. Whether you are religious or not, Notre Dame Cathedral is a place that makes one slow down, contemplate, and appreciate culture and history more deeply.

The Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral (also known as Saigon Cathedral or Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon) is an iconic landmark located in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. Built by the French between 1877 and 1883, the cathedral’s neo-Romanesque architectural style stands as a testament to the city's colonial past and remains one of the most prominent symbols of Saigon (now Ho Chi Minh City).  Architecture and Aesthetic Appeal The cathedral's most striking feature is its elegant façade, made of red bricks imported from France. Its twin 58-meter-high towers dominate the skyline, and the cathedral’s central rose window adds a touch of grandeur. The design, inspired by French Gothic architecture, incorporates elements like pointed arches, flying buttresses, and ribbed vaults. Inside, the cathedral is just as captivating, with stained-glass windows depicting biblical scenes and a serene atmosphere that contrasts with the bustling city outside.  The exterior of the cathedral is equally impressive. With a large open space in front, it creates a peaceful area amidst the city's hustle and bustle, making it a popular spot for both locals and tourists to relax and take photos.  Historical Significance The Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral holds deep historical and cultural importance. During the French colonial period, it was built to serve the growing Catholic community in the region. Over the years, it has witnessed numerous historical events and remains an active place of worship today. The cathedral is not just a religious site but also an essential piece of Vietnam’s history and heritage.  Visitor Experience Visiting the Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral is an enriching experience, both for those interested in architecture and history. The cathedral’s proximity to other landmarks like the Central Post Office and Ben Thanh Market makes it an easy addition to a day of sightseeing in the city center.  For tourists, the exterior of the cathedral is generally open to the public, offering plenty of opportunities for photos and leisurely exploration. However, the interior is often closed to visitors outside of service times, so it’s advisable to check ahead or visit during mass to fully experience the building’s sacred atmosphere. The cathedral also hosts regular religious services, which are open to the public, providing a chance to witness the local Catholic community in action.  Cultural Significance The cathedral remains a popular site for both religious observances and cultural events. It is also a gathering point for local Catholics, especially on important holidays like Christmas and Easter. Additionally, the Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral stands as a symbol of the city’s diverse heritage, where both Catholics and non-Catholics alike come to admire its beauty and historical depth.  Conclusion The Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral is a must-see for anyone visiting Ho Chi Minh City. Whether you're an architecture lover, a history enthusiast, or simply someone who appreciates the beauty of a well-maintained landmark, this cathedral offers something for everyone. Its peaceful ambiance and striking beauty make it an unforgettable destination in the heart of one of Vietnam's most dynamic cities.

In downtown Ho Chi Minh City, an important remnant of the French colonial period in Vietnam - the "Cathedral Basilica of Our Lady of the Immaculate Conception", most commonly known as "Notre-Dame Cathedral". It was built, between 1863 and 1880, by French Roman Catholics and was simply known as the "Church of Saigon". It is a neo-romanesque construction, with an all-red brick facade, two outstanding bell towers, six bronze bells and beautiful stained-glass windows. In 1959, a granite statue of Virgin Mary, depicted as "Our Lady of Peace", was brought from Italy and placed at "Paris Square", also known as "Notre-Dame Square", which is right in front of the ancient church. Immediately after, the "Church of Saigon" was designated as the city's Cathedral. It has been undergoing severe restoration works and has been closed to visitors for already a long time. Stroll along and try to imagine how beautiful it certainly is, a true landmark of the Roman Catholic presence in Southeast Asia. Enjoy!

As a famous landmark in the city center, the church is a must-see for tourists to visit and admire its architecture and scenery.
I was truly overwhelmed by the beauty of this sacred, ancient place with a distinct Western feel—a wonderful place to visit and find peace.
The place was under construction but looks still great. Most people came here were tourist and unfortunately we couldn’t get inside to see

I visited Ho Chi Minh 3 times...only got 1 chance to enter the Notre other 2 trips its close for renovation i guess. Its taking too long i guess....i know its old building...but with the technology we should go fast.Dont miss Biu Vien street if ur a party goer....great place to be for music.
The colonial-style architecture is delicate and beautiful; every angle is photogenic. It's a shame I visited during renovations. The entire building is truly stunning. I must visit again sometime.

The cathedral was constructed between 1863 and 1880. All original building materials were imported from France. Two bell towers were added to the cathedral in 1895, the whole structure since lasts to date.
Presently undergoing major overhaul and is restricted for access... It's in the heart of the city and a very picturesque location... You can spend a nice evening here
A very butifull church. Don't miss it. It's very nice to see it in a very different area and arc. If you have a little time get inside.

This religious landmark is located in District 1, surrounded by greenery and skyscrapers. Since I'm not religious, I just walked around the church and took pictures. It's a sacred place I respect, one of the few remaining in District 1, with a Western-style church design.
If you come to Saigon, you absolutely must visit this place as it's a landmark of the city. Besides, there are many other historical sites nearby that are just a short walk away.

It’s Vietnamese Name: Nha Tho Duc Ba. For centuries, the building is a landmark for Saigon Center History that all couples prays for a romantic relationship. As others French architecture, it brings Western image for an Asian colonial city.

One of Saigon's iconic buildings, a constant target for selfies among tourists, this red-brick cathedral with its two towering twin spires sits in an open square across from the Central Post Office. Because it's open-air, it's best to visit in the morning or afternoon. The weather can be quite hot. A white Regina Pacis statue stands tall in front of the church. There's also a small park nearby. Unfortunately, the park and church were undergoing renovations when we visited. Many of the supporting pillars were missing, which somewhat detracted from the beauty of the photos. Hopefully, the renovations will be completed soon.
It's under construction and you can't go inside. You can go and take a picture from the outside and visit the post office, which is a very nice building.
Most enjoyable as part of our city tour, even though it’s under renovation. Put it on your list in Ho Chi Minh
Excellent and Historical Cathedral in South East Asia of which almost match the original in Paris, France.
The cathedral was under renovation when we visited in July 2019. However, I have been inside before and it is a definite must see if you are in Saigon.
Notre Dame Cathedral in Saigon is a faint reminder of Notre Dame de Paris. After exploring it, I was left with only a faint impression.
The Cathedral in Ho Chi Minh City. Large and beautiful. Many foreign tourists visit, even if only to take photos.
One of the most crowded area in the city Should you be here on the weekend. The street is very active and interesting
Beautiful cathedral. Unfortunately you cannot go inside unless you attend their mass, and cannot walk out during the service Must stay for the whole mass.
It's really beautiful, but it was under construction in July 2019. I recommend going to see it because it's very pretty, at least what I saw. The post office is right next door.

Although I am not a Catholic, I have been there several times. Whenever, I visit Saigon I like to walk around this Cathedral. The ambiance in and out of it is great. I like the way it tries to emulate the Gothic architecture of original Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris. You can admire its beauty while sipping unique Vietnamese coffee from a nearby coffee shop. However, be careful with the touts hanging around the cathedral. They try hard to sell things like drugs to sex, if they see you as a tourist.

Last summer when I was here, it was completely closed. This year, it is open for Mass, which is held many times a day -but not open for tourists ...so make sure to dress accordingly! A couple of the Masses are in English, but I forgot which ones. Much of the scaffolding that was there last year is gone, affording much better views of the beautiful building.
Quiet and surrounded by greenery, it's very cool in the mornings, and in the evenings you'll find sidewalk cafes and young people playing guitar. It's truly special.
This is building is under renovation but still is a beautiful building with alot of historical significance.

The cathedral was under construction so we were only able to view the exterior. But it still didn’t take away the intricate architectural style from the French colonial time. Also, the cathedral is located next to the Central Post Office so we also went there afterwards.

Was able to hear mass in this lovely Cathedral. We were seated near the fan and it helped because it can be hot inside. One can take plenty of nice pictures outside. There are many tourists in this area, but they don't allow people to go inside when the service starts so be early if you plan to hear mass.

Visited the church for Sunday mass at 930am. It is pretty hot inside so sit near a fan, or dress light. Ladies : cover your shoulders, and your knees. They initially said no sandals but I saw a few people in them so maybe not so strict about footwear if they are decent. Men : best to also cover the shoulders and knees. Beautiful building.
It's a classic tourist attraction, but they were renovating when I went, so it was crowded. Going in the evening is nice and not too hot. Overall, it's beautiful.

Although I couldn't go inside because it was under construction, I felt a sense of solemnity from the church's exterior. Perhaps it's the prayers for peace offered by so many people that evoke that feeling. I would like to visit again after the renovations are complete.
It really nice well maintained place, Nice architecture, we couldn't enter seem it close for renovation. Nice photo shot spot.

Notre Dame Cathedral is under construction now but in the past I had a chance to look inside. Beautiful stained glass window with 2 peak towers. A peaceful Virgin Mary outside. Would come back to visit again!
Stayed 3months in Saigon, and walked thru this church close to the post office was a very beautiful with french architecture.

Unfortunately under renovation on my visit but still nice to look at. A statue of the mother Mary is in the square which makes for a very nice picture, and worshipping if you believe that nonsense.  We couldn’t get into the building but we took a walk around the outside studying the architecture. I like European Architecture so this was a good thing for me to visit.
Such a beautiful Church, one of my favourite things to see in such a busy place, Has a calming effect amongst all the hustle and bussle. Great architecture.
This is the most beauty place for me in Ho Chi Minh city. Should visit Peace and nice almostphere Alot of activities around this area

Notre Dame Cathedral boasts a beautiful exterior and is one of Ho Chi Minh City's landmark buildings. There's also a large square opposite the cathedral, perfect for taking great photos. Unfortunately, the cathedral was still under renovation during my visit, so I couldn't go inside.
We had just 2 days in Saigon and this was a great place to visit, a smaller copy of the famous one in France Well worth a look as part of a tour
A church that slightly emulates the Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris, a vestige of the French colonial era.

Currently, the site is only accessible for photo ops from the outside. Reconstruction is currently in its second year and is scheduled for completion no earlier than the end of 2023. French organizations, which built the church over 130 years ago, are participating in the work. A replica of the Notre Dame de Paris, the church is located on Place de la Comune de Paris, at the beginning of Saigon's most expensive street, Dong Hoi.

The cathedral was filled with worshippers of all ages; we attended Mass in Vietnamese last Sunday! What fervor, what respect, what faith! The choir was absolutely fantastic—that's an understatement... We loved it... Some might say that tourists can't visit the inside of the cathedral during the renovations, which is why, after being turned away by a vehement guard for fifteen minutes, a very kind Vietnamese man made him understand that we wanted to participate in the entire service! A thousand thanks 🙏

Well maintained and well known old Church. Unfortunately, we were not allowed to walk-in to the church due to renovation work. However, we were able to take some pictures from outside. One should not miss this Church. Awesome feeling!

Beautiful and historic, still under renovation but opens for daily mass at 5:30AM (Vietnamese only). Don't miss the large catholic bookstore across the road, vast amount of articles, even though books are mostly in Vietnamese.
It was a place where you could feel history in an instant. Since it's in the city center, you can easily visit even if you're short on time. There weren't too many people there.
A fun destination for young people to meet up on weekends. The "floor-seating" cafe has a unique Saigon characteristic that can't be found anywhere else.

It was under renovation during our visit and so sad,, we didn’t got a chance to offer a prayer, but still blessed to witness this architectural cathedral. Hope to give another visit when it’s fully constructed!
Unfortunately it was not possible to visit as the whole church was under big renovation . Hopefully next time I visit Saigon that lovely city!
Cathedral was closed but just being outside was an amazing experience to see such perfection in Architecture.
Place of Catholic worship for Vietnamese and tourists from all over the world. It gets very crowded on Sundays and access is limited.
Beautiful church, currently being renovated (May 2019). You can only photograph it from the outside. The post office is practically next door.
The ancient building is unique with the red wall and so heautiful. it acts an icon for saigon city. Every tourist must come and see this beautiful building.
The cathedral currently is under renovation. So visitors are not able to go inside..  But still able to take a photo at the front of the cathedral. Recommend go after 3pm, less crowed.

There is a bookstore street and a McDonald's nearby. There are also many cafes and street food stalls, so it was nice to be able to take photos and enjoy myself freely. There were also a lot of people who came to take pictures.
The most striking features are the imposing church towers and the large statue of Mary. This statue was brought here from Rome in 1959.
superb architecture and green space forecourt leads one into this lovely catholic cathedral. somewhat unusual to see a church of this significance in Asia but worth the stop

A place that everyone admires and cherishes. Ancient, solemn, and majestic. Cool and airy, yet still feeling warm and friendly. If you haven't had the chance to visit, you should definitely come once. I've been here many times already, but I still want to come again. Whenever I have the opportunity, I'll come right away. Thank you, Notre Dame Cathedral.
We hope that Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral will be soon completed the renovation. There are always a lot of tourists who come to see this cathedral

Unfortunately, I went to take a look today, but it's covered in scaffolding! If you're reading this, and it's still May 2019, you might want to check how far along they are before you grab a Grab or walk yourself into the ground. It remains a beautiful building, though, hence the good rating.

Lovely cathedral right in the heart of Saigon ..but massive reconstruction and repair happening .. expected to re open to public end of 2019.. outside is a massive mobs of tour groups. As it is opposite the post office ... you do not need a tour for this you can walk easily from vimton shopping mall or Saigon centre or ban than market all are 5 mins or so... and capture 2 of saigons best known features without paying 100 dollars and upwards for a tour ...if you walk past the McDonald’s (you can’t miss it) there is a street full of coffee and book shops to round off your own tour

A beautiful church in a very busy and also pretty location near the Independence Palace and a lovely park. In this place, you can feel a little of the Saigon atmosphere from the movies. Beautiful women with fair skin, majestic dresses, and incredible hats stroll everywhere. I loved the square in front, where there's a fantastic place to buy souvenirs. A must-see in Ho Chi Minh City.
A beautiful place. We visited in the evening, and it was beautifully lit. We couldn't see inside, as it was closed.

Easy to get to if staying in District One. I have now visited the church many times and still find it a calm and peaceful escape after every visit.  It is also a great place for photos too. Not just the building but also the local activities. There are often brides and grooms having photo shoots as well as other activities.  While no charge is needed, I am always happy to make a donation. I look forward to seeing it again after the latest batch of renovations.
Very nice cathedral! I recommend everyone who is in the city to visit this 🤗 Wasn't possible to go inside when I was there.

This is one of the most important cathedrals in all of Southeast Asia. It was built during the colonial era at the end of the 19th century. It stands 58 meters tall, and in front of its entrance, in a small park, is a statue of the Virgin Mary.
The place is currently under the renovation process, if you know what I mean, just come and visit quickly!

Unfortunately we could not visit inside and the outside was under construction - so could not take any pics bug a beautifully designed building. Great to be able see it. I think the recons is taking very long as it is sponsored by catholic people in Saigon. Saw it during our Saigon City tour
Currently under renovation, we could only look from the outside but it’s worth a look if your in the area.
I was SO sad it was under construction while I was on my visit in Vietnam. But the outside..... words or pictures don’t even do it justice. It is a beautiful cathedral.

Another historic places you must visited is the grand church... your visiting will not be complete without this historic places.. locating near the historic post office at the centre of Ho Chi Ming City
Visiting the church on a beautiful August day. The interior of the church is incredibly beautiful and ancient. The windows, which look hundreds of years old, evoke memories of the past.
only got to see the outside of the building as it was close for construction now, just next to the post office and McDonald next to it too April 2019
With its beautiful architecture and a history spanning over 300 years alongside the people of Saigon, the church is currently undergoing repairs, including roof tile replacement...
Good place to visit just across the central post office. You cannot enter though. It srands beautifully on the main road
The cathedral is being renovated, but you can still view it from the outside. It only takes about 10 minutes to walk around the building
Great short tour , cheeky but very friendly guide “Laughing” nice to see places through a locals commentary. A very enjoyable 4 hours. Stil LAUGHING !
Beautiful architecture near the City Post Office. Currently under renovation. Located right in the city center, making it very easy to visit.
Stunning view of the Cathedral. I happened to arrive when they performed a mass and it was so enchanting and beautiful.
The back is under construction, but it was very beautiful! It resembled Notre Dame Cathedral in France! I couldn't go inside, but I'm sure the stained glass and other things inside are beautiful ☆

NOTRE- DAME CATHEDRAL BASILICA OF SAIGON Vietnamese: Vương cung thánh đường Chính tòa Đức Bà Sài Gòn or Nhà thờ Đức Bà Sài Gòn.  We went to Sunday mass this morning. The English service started at 9:30am. The cathedral is located in the downtown of Ho Chi Minh City.  The service was breathtaking.💖 My 16 years of going to St Frannies in Frankston kicked in.Lol My cherubs were curious by the Catholic rituals (ie: stand up, sit down, stand up, sit down, stand up, kneel, sit, kneel, stand... lol. etc)  Established by French colonists who initially named it Cathédrale Notre-Dame de Saïgon, the cathedral was constructed between 1863 and 1880. It has two bell towers, reaching a height of 58 meters.  The building is under construction. Work has been going for over 2 years. We were fortunate to be able to spend time inside the building during the service. It is off limits outside of mass hours.
This Cathedral is located in the central city. Gothic architech. English mass on Sunday morning closed by the central post office and department stores. 15 minutes walk to city hall and opera house..
The cathedral was impressive,but unfortunately could not enter it due to the repair works going on. However got some good photos of the front.

On March 16th, the area around the church was under renovation. If you're planning to go there, I recommend the nearby century-old post office, which is also quite nice. When visiting the area, please be mindful of your belongings; it's always good to be cautious when traveling.
Parts of the structure were undergoing some constructions when I visited. Nevertheless the magnificent building still shines. A different feel compared to Norte Dam in Paris....

This is a neat old Roman Catholic Church that has a lot of history. It is currently under repair on the exterior. Being Catholic we worshipped there on Ash Wednesday. Every seat was occupied by the congregation.  During services the church restricts access to those attending mass.

We were disappointed that we could not enter the Cathedral but it was being refurbished. The Cathedral is a fantastic looking building from the outside even though it’s being refurbished and we really enjoyed seeing it.

It is in front of the central post office , so you will be able to see two attractions at the same spot. People were praying here and on the opposite road some were feeding pigeons . Go in the evening and spend some time , know about the place. You will encounter people who were affected in Vietnamese war are selling stuff there and you will feel proud about them seeing their hardwork.

Otherwise also "Nha tho Duc Ba Sai Gon". Closed in February 2019 due to ongoing reconstruction. However, if travelers are interested in French colonial remains in Saigon, they should not miss this Christian building. It stands on the square of the same name and was built by the French in 1883. And also a short distance from it is the fantastic building of the main post office. It is really worth seeing, the atmosphere is still unrepeatable. I recommend it.
Gustave Eiffel's wonder in the east can be seen and admired in Saigon. No wonder maximum visitors to Saigon are from France followed by Chinese

This is a lovely place.. We had are wedding pictures taken here 10.years ago. But unfortunately at the moment ho Chi Minh City is being redeveloped due to the underground railway being installed so at this moment you will not be able to see much as it is boarded up....

Walking around Notre Dame Cathedral, the quiet, ancient beauty is breathtaking. The old architecture enhances the church's solemnity, adding to Ho Chi Minh City's charm with a beautiful, ancient, and unique architectural masterpiece! I will definitely return here soon!
An iconic landmark of Ho Chi Minh City. A must-see destination for tourists visiting Vietnam.

We saw it during the trip organized by Elena of Vietnamstay to Vietnam, Cambodia, and Myanmar. It was closed for renovations. When we next passed through Saigon, when we returned from Myanmar, the work was finishing and it was open. It's worth a visit, along with the nearby Central Post Office.
Beautiful and Majestic. The Cathedral is a marvel to gaze upon. Located just next to the Post office in District 1. One can even attend mass in the early am. Please do check the timings for entry.
This is the place that you have to visit when you are in HCM city. It's near independence palace and really big green park!

It was truly moving to participate in Mass in this historic place. There is an English Mass at 9:30 AM on Sundays. Even for non-believers, it's worth a visit, as the church's exterior is magnificent, and the statue of the Virgin Mary in the front courtyard is exquisite.
You can't miss a visit to this wonderful cathedral...imposing...immense...it can be visited in its entirety except for the central part.
Currently closed for restoration work. However, the esplanade is a great spot for photos.

Beautiful from the outside. We are in district 1 and can walk to most sights. No need for a tour really. The cathedral is closed for visits at the moment but open for mass at around 5 pm. - in Vietnamese.. There is the beautiful building of the post office next to it where one can buy souvenirs and do any post office transactions.
We attended the 5:30 pm mass on Sunday at Notre Dame Cathedral in Saigon. The religiosity of Vietnamese Catholics was surprising.
What a great pity that this site was closed for renovations when we were in Ho Chi Minh City in October / November 2018. But you never know, we may return in the future.
Nice place to make a photos. Easy to reach. Close to central post. Must see object. Not more then 20 min spend...Only coz crowd to make a photo
Beautiful, fresh air, best to visit in the morning, you can come here to pray. However, it is currently under renovation and will be open again as soon as possible.

Very beautiful building and was very striking in the centre of the city. Great place to visit and also very close to other monuments and places of interest. Plenty of cafes and restaurants to sit at around the cathedral also.

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral Closed for renovation when we were there. I imagine it is a beautiful building from the French colonial era, as the name suggests. Many people get married there when it is open. What is most impressive is its location next to the Post Office and the place where the church is located, a square frequented by both Saigon residents and tourists, which makes it a tourist attraction. The statue in front of the cathedral is very beautiful, I think. Definitely worth a visit!

My 2nd visit to the Cathedral. They are still busy with renovations. Took different photos this time. Its good to do a lot of research before you visit. Though one can't go in, its still so worth seeing.
Good place to meditate or.attemd Sunday mass. Loving history in the center of this lively city. Old colonial architecture tells stories of a world long gone.

We visited Notre Dame Cathedral on Christmas day. However, due to renovation we could not go inside. Our guide told us to attend Saturday evening or Sunday morning mass if we want to see inside. According to him the church is not accessible during other times due to the current renovation. The cathedral was built by the French when Vietnam was a French colony. According to our guide, the French government is sponsoring the current renovation project and will be completed by late next year or early 2021. We attended Sunday mass at 9.30 am on December 30th. If the outside look impressive, then the inside is even better with impressive paintings and French architecture. It was certainly worth the visit.
One of these buildings from the colonial era. A magnificent structure that must be seen. Magnificent and magnificent.

Inspite of the bombings, etc, it is really overwhelming to see the majestic cathedral standing tall.It has a fair share of glorious history and we soaked in it. History apart, it is a must visit site in Saigon. Do not forget to click pictures !!

The church is very impressive from the outside. Unfortunately, it is currently closed for restoration work. The exterior walls are completely covered in scaffolding, but the view of the church from the front is unobstructed.

This cathedral could be anywhere in Europe and it would still be a significant example of 19th Century French Architecture and Catholic doctrine. It's a beautiful building and truly worth the stop...!!!
We went there twice but couldn’t get in. Plenty of activity outside with photographers and models. Lovely at night time. Right next to the Post Office.

A magnificent building in the heart of bustling Saigon, beautiful from the outside. Looking at it, you temporarily forget you're in Asia and mentally transport yourself to Europe, oblivious to the bustling crowds, the huge stream of mopeds, and the hustle and bustle of city life. We visited the inside on a previous trip several years ago and loved it: it was calm and tranquil, just as a cathedral should be.

Make sure you at least pass and see the cathedral. Recommend exploring Saigon on foot and seeing everything you can. Easy to see this and Independence Palace whilst walking around. Beautiful building.

It's currently not accepting visitors due to renovation but if you're a Catholic just let them know you wan to join the mass if there is one on going. Make sure you're dressed proper to enter. English Mass is at 5.30pm on Sunday and 4.30pm for vietnamese Mass.  It was a blessing in disguise for an opportunity to join in the mass as the choir sang heavenly. No pictures in the church too bad.

I was there first thing in the morning as we were on a schedule. It’s a lovely looking church & last time I was here - October- it was closed for refurbishment of the interior. It was shut at 7.30 am but looked like it would be open later. Next to the Post Office which is a HCMC must do also. It’s a 10 minute trot round the exterior I’ve not done the interior for a while but I’d guess 15-30 mins depending on how interested you are.

The church was under renovations during our visit in late December 2018, and it was closed for the public. However, even the outer appearance of the building speaks loads about the fine architecture of the structure.

Notre Dame Cathedral is my favorite place in Saigon. It's a very ancient and solemn building. I've attended Mass there a few times; the interior architecture is beautiful, and the priests' sermons are excellent. However, I don't like seeing some young people dressed too revealingly and taking pictures right at the church gate or near the statue of the Virgin Mary. It doesn't look polite or respectful.
I've been coming to Saigon for five years now. Every time I'm fascinated by the beautiful church. This time, however, it's under reconstruction. Always worth a visit.
I absolutely loved visiting Notre Dame Cathedral. It's so beautiful and incredibly warm, you have to go there! I completely lost track of the present for a while.

I visited this cathedral at evening and it was under renovation activity , and couldnt check in , the exterior or the cathedral is a perfect example of Gothic architecture in Saigon , you can visit the Heritage building of Saign Post office with french colonial architecture, still in use today. The tiny garden in front of the cathedral adds elegant to this marvelous red building.

Date of visit : 17 Dec 2018  Time of visit : 12 noon  We only took some pictures there, prayed but could not enter the cathedral as it was under major restoration. If you walk closed enough, you will see the Nativity before the cathedral put up due to Christmas.  The small Notre Dame square where Mother Mary statue stood had beautiful flower beds. There were a number of tourists (unsure from which country) stood on the flower beds and immediately guards blew whistle.  After visiting Notre Dame cathedral, we crossed the road and arrived at The Central Post Office.
Yes, it's a Parisian-style cathedral. To the right is a McDonald's and a post office selling local souvenirs, interesting, but I don't think they let you inside.

A very beautiful building with stunning architecture. Just that it was under renovation at the time of my visit so really couldn’t get it. One of the must do sight seeing spots when in Saigon. Also very centrally locates just adjacent to the Post Office which is also a must visit. Just fascinating.
Stand out structure defining colonial period architecture. Centrally located and many interesting cafes nearby!
Notre Dame de Saigon is different from the French Notre Dame de Paris, but it's truly very interesting! We got there during a wedding ceremony, and we loved it.
You can take photos in front of the facade, but you can't go inside yet. Reconstruction has been ongoing for two years, since 2017.
A beautiful building standing proud in its place in Ho Chi Minh. Due to a local blessing day we were unable to look inside!
Old and well kept. Church was closed as it was going thru some reno. Still can take pics outside and cross over to central post office.

One of Saigon's proudest landmarks. An ancient cathedral, a spiritual refuge. Every time I come here, I feel joyful and peaceful. Thinking of downtown Saigon brings to mind the image of Notre Dame Cathedral.
We attended the English mass on Sunday at 9.30am at this magnificent cathedral with amazing architecture. So captivated by the lovely voices of the choir too.

We went to See the Cathedral but was Disappointed that it was Closed currently for renovations. They did have some nice Pictures of the Cathedral around the construction Fence around the Cathedral and Christmas Decorations, but Disappointed Closed. I could not understand Why the Church Closed when it looked like the Outside being done.
Took picture with the birds..but the church was closed undergoing renovation....not much activity here ..since i am a catholic this is a mabdatory place to visit..
We were unable to go inside because of the renovations but, even from the outside, it was possible to see the magnificence of the architecture and the unusual pink colour

Under construction... Let's not expect the grandeur of European cathedrals in Vietnam. It was quaint and charming. I couldn't go inside, presumably because a mass was being held, so I just lingered nearby and took photos.

A historical building of worship located in the centre of Ho Chi minh City. I managed to attend the Sunday Mass in English at 930am the local time. It was a great experience to be there for participating in the Eucharistic celebration together with the wonderful choirs
This building is a small copy same as Church at France. Very beautiful and lot of people come here every day to take pictures.

This church is now close for renovation however you can still take picture at the front of the church which is still really pretty. It is located opposite the post office hence you can take the time to nail two spots at one go.  However beware of the drinks seller or vendors that offers to help you to take pictures. Yes they are helpful but in the end they will ask you for tips or payments when they voluntarily offered to take picture for you. So just be aware.

We call on the cathedral just before 25/12/18, but were advised that it was closed to the public. However, it was open on 25/12/18, and we were privileged to have been able to enjoy the Christmas Church service. It is well worth the visit.
it is located near the art deco post office , the only problem is that it seems to be closed much of the time i went like 4 time different days & hours ... but closed
So stunning. A little bit of history, and very beautiful. They were busy with renovations and we couldn't go in. But the outside is amazing. Don't miss this when in Saigon.

Centrally located with surrounded by hectic city pace, yet felt the solitude and calm when in the cathedral. English service on Sundays is at 9.30am has many nationalities. Currently exterior is under renovation and some parts may not look good for pictures.

We went there. This cathedral is sublime. It was very crowded. It's a shame we could only see it from the outside because it was being renovated. Imagine a cathedral like ours in Asia. No, it's beautiful and so full of history and emotion.

Was a pity and the place was closed for Renovation and even the tour guide could not tell us when renovation would complete.. you can still take beautiful shots of this place from a distance like wat I did

Ok it's the chathidral you must see it . But it's better when it have a service. More over this period , to be except some monts before until now they do some construction or renovation. I think in christmas will be a good period to have a visit.

A wonderful piece of architecture. Under repairs at the moment, however you still get to see its beauty and you feel the warmth as you get closer to it. An amazing setting for photo's. It will be even better once the fine and slow detailed repairs are completed.

Don't go to HCMC without visit Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral. It is a must if you want to see the French architecture during the occupation. Remember that many Vietnamese are Catholic due to a French priest that help to create the Vietnamese alphabet in roman letters. Look closely to designs and patterns, and you will be delighted with such rich art. Enjoy it as I did!
It is identical to the Notre Dame in Paris. The French influence is everywhere in this beautiful HCM city or Saigon.
This cathedral is unique in architecture and amazing monument , it's so beautiful from inside as u feel so calm and find peace within themselves.

Combine this with a visit to the post office which is right next door, take a photo, feed the pigeons, get an ice cream and enjoy it in the square; in the morning, many newlyweds come here to have their wedding photos taken.
Beautiful place of historical importance. Must visit place in city center and close to many major attractions of the city.
I was totally amaze by looking at the facade of this church. It's like France was here. 😊 I felt relaxed and overwhelmed too.
A slightly smaller replica of the Paris cathedral, unfortunately currently enclosed by a tin fence due to renovations. Well worth seeing.

Besides being a prominent spot for tourists visiting Saigon, or a place where families and young people come every evening... the special thing here is the choir's beautiful hymn singing, which left the most lasting impression on me when attending Mass.

This is definitely a copy of the Notre Dame in PARIS!! Having seen the real one and this, I truly believe that the French colony has done a great job in Vietnam. Unfortunately I wasn't able to enter the church due to construction work that is going on so I was able to view them from the outside only.

Even though the cathedral is undergoing renovations currently, we were lucky enough to attend mass here. What an awesome experience!! No photos were allowed so I can only tell you how beautiful it is inside. I would love to see it again once they have finished them. Such a wonderful honour to see this and experience a service in Vietnamese.
It has a beautiful exterior, but it is currently under construction, which is a shame. Located just across the street from the post office, it is a must-visit spot during a city tour. I recommend it.
One of the best landmarks to see and absorb the appealing surroundings and structure. A must to visit

Amidst the bustling traffic, the church appears remarkably tranquil. The afternoon sun further enhances its beauty, giving it its nickname, the "Red Church." Unfortunately, it's currently under renovation and closed to the public.

Notre-Dame Cathedral was given after Bishop Pham Van Thien held a ceremony to install the statue of Peaceful Notre Dame, made with granite from Rome, in 1959. In 1962, Vatican anointed it as Saigon Chief Cathedral conferred it basilique. Since this time, this cathedral is called Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica.
A virtual replica of the Parisian cathedral, it is located in the center of Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon), adjacent to the modern buildings of Vietnam's largest city.

It's hard to believe that the French felt the need to bring all the materials, everything, all the way from France to build this cathedral. The result is Europe in Vietnam. Per our guide it is currently closed for renovations, but they apparently still have Mass. Would have liked to get inside.
A must see with Cricket Motorbike Tour! Learn about the history of Saigon. Beautiful architecture! And the bells rang while we were there! Stunning experience.

I was roaming around following the free city map picked up from my hotel lobby.  The name attracted - me as I was a student of prestigious Notre Dame college in Dhaka!  I like the architecture of the cathedral very much. Although renovation was going on, it did not stop tourists like me to take a few snaps.

This is actually a very famous place in Ho Chi Minh City. It's a distinctive French architectural masterpiece that still exists today. Tourists with time can visit and take photos as souvenirs. If it's your first time in Ho Chi Minh City, don't miss this fascinating place.
It's a beautiful church and it is very big inside. I am told they discourage people from taking photos inside.  They have English masses.
The old church, with its distinctly French architecture, has stood for a very long time in Saigon. However, it is currently undergoing restoration.
The cathedral is under repair on one side, but it is worth a look. Beautiful architecture, and the statue of Maria at the front is awe enspiring
I recommend u to visit this place. Every foreigner come HCM city, they pass by here as a symbol. U should be here with a group of friends.
Romanesque Architecture at its very best...but covered now for renovation..still a great experience..
We took some photos in here from the outside of the church. We must take the line to took some photos. A lot of peoples want to take some photo too. In the same spot.
