This is a fantastic place to visit although now surrounded by the likes of McDonalds (such a shame), it is still a beautiful place to take some pictures & a stroll.
At the time of this review the cathedral is under maintenance. The building and surrounding gardens are beautiful and compliment each other.

Mentioning Notre Dame Cathedral, everyone knows it; every Saigon resident knows it by heart. Located in District 1, the city center, it attracts many domestic and international tourists who come to visit and pray. With its French architecture, the cathedral has a classic yet modern look, making it a favorite spot for young people to visit, take photos, and capture beautiful moments.

The Cathedral is currently undergoing major restoration works, so can only go inside if attending a mass, which we did so we could go in. As it is only open for mass, you can't take any photos inside. It is situated right across the road from the General Post Office, so I'd recommend you go to see both as they really are must see's if you are visiting Ho Chi Minh City.
It's an old colonial building... You cannot take pics inside the church but outside you are allowed. It's a beautiful church indeed. Worth a visit

If this church was for example in France of Italy, perhaps it would have been a typical Catholic Church . However in the center of Saigon a city of Vietnam the sight of the statue of Virgin Mary and the gothic church in the background is something special and unique that is definitely worth seeing .

Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon, officially Cathedral Basilica of Our Lady of the Immaculate Conception is located in the downtown of Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. Established by |French colonists who initially named it Cathedrale Notre-Dame de Saigon, it was constructed between 1863 and 1880. It has two bell towers, reaching a height of 58 meters.  All building materials were imported from France; the outside wall of the cathedral was built with bricks from Toulouse. Although the contractor did not use coated concrete, these bricks have retained their bright red color until today.  In the flower garden in front of the cathedral, there waqs a bronze statue of Pigneau de Behaine leading Prince Canh, the son of Emperor Gia Long by his right hand. The statue was made in France.  As Catholics, we were thankful that we were able to visit this Cathedral although we were unable to enter because there was refurbishment going on. We silently said our prayers near the door for blessings and guidance for the whole 12 days of journey.

It was astonishing and heart-warming to find a magnificent catholic cathedral in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City. The Cathedral is now under extensive renovation, but is still open to public for services and prayers! The main altar was simple but the side chapels are really well ornate with different themes! Worshippers certainly find peace and comfort in this holy ground. A Must for all Christians!
It is a smaller scale of the notre Dame cathedral in Paris . According to our tour guide, every single brick and material are shipped from France . They are recently under renovation
Lovely area for a walk in the heart of district one. Walk up through the pedestrian street at night to see it all lit up.

This is the oldest church in Ho Chi Minh City. The building is unique & beautiful, Many tourists come to take selfies here. It's great, you should come here,, The city is clean & busy. Many traders can speak Indonesian 👍👏
Beautiful church, fortunately attend Sunday mass here,so entry was possible otherwise some renovations happening so limited entry
It’s a beautiful place and show a bit about the French influence in their culture and with the catholic religion.

Ground zero in Saigon!!! A place worth any length of walk :) I was most impressed by the late evening/night atmosphere near the cathedral – tons of pubs around, each packed with people. I HIGHLY RECOMMEND IT TO EVERYONE :)))
I go there with my son. The place it's good. It's near Central Post Office. When I coming the place is under renovation. But it's ok. Need crop to good picture.

Went to see the Cathedral but it was closed due to restoration you can see from the outside but half the building is covered with scaffolding and green cloth .what a pity as the part you can see looks like you would like to go inside and have a look.
The Cathedral is undergoing a huge renovation on its exterior, but Masses are still celebrated on schedule. Music was uplifting and the 9AM English Mass on Sunday was pleasantly packed.
Unfortunately we couldn't see inside due to restoration works, however the facade is still visible and makes for lovely photo opportunity.

Ho CHi Minh city is young and dynamic but Notre Dame. It is quiet and ancient and we were very impressed with its architecture and beauty. It became the symbol of Saigon and I like to take some photos with this too :)

Portions of the Cathedral was under renovation and when there was choir practice the access was closed. Despite that, it was nice to listen to the choir practicing while adoring the beauty of the architecture of the Cathedral from outside.
Lovely to see but unfortunately it's closed for renovation. A beautiful building though. I'm not sure when it will be open to the public again, but great area too.

Only a few steps across the road from the Saigon Central Post Office,, While we did not go in to the Bascillica we did manage to photograph this wonderful building even though it is under renovations at the moment, well worth seeing and taking a walk across the street to the Saigon Central Post Office
Right opposite the Central Post Office.  Beautifully built and being restored. It can be seen from most parts of District 1.  A heritage from when France was in power.

A lot of Newly Weds and their Invitees go here to be photographed. When I was here (Apr. 2018)the Church was undergoing reconstruction but it was still a favorite stop for them. Also visit the Post Office across the street.
Beautiful Cathedral worth visiting. Well worth your time. Gives a good history of Catholicism in a country like Vietnam. Great location for other sights as well.
Really nice to stroll around and take photos. Getting a good shot of people and cathedral is fun! It’s one of the many places so easily reached on foot!

Very nice church. Some vendors selling stuff for a cheap price. Worth a visit. It’s also in front of the post office. Their tourist spots are near each other so you don’t have to spend too much for transportation since you can reach them by walking.
Of course, Ho Chi Minh City wouldn't be Ho Chi Minh City without its French-made cathedral... an iconic and unmissable landmark... currently undergoing renovations

Located in the heart of the city, amidst a constant flow of traffic, this cathedral, reminiscent of Notre Dame de Paris in its reimagined Romanesque-Gothic style, was built around 1880 using red brick from Toulouse. Currently undergoing renovations, access is prohibited. I highly recommend taking a moment to pause here, as it is, along with the post office and the opera house, one of the last major vestiges of the French period. The majority of buildings and houses reflecting the colonial era have been demolished over the past five years to make way for the new and modern Ho Chi Minh City.
The church is close to the central post office, so after visiting here, you can stop by the central post office. There are many souvenir sellers here, you can bargain to get a cheap price.

It's very beautiful, but you can only admire and take photos from the outside. Entry inside is prohibited. You should bring an umbrella or hat because the sun is quite hot. If you want to capture every angle in your photos, I recommend crossing the street to the opposite side; you'll be able to get pictures from all angles.
No entrance fees An instagrammable spot for anyone who looking for it. Many couples use the spot to shoot a romantic scenes.
The cathedral is undergoing renovations and is not currently open to the public. However, it is possible to attend a service. A beautiful red brick church; from a distance, it resembles Toulouse...

We visited this as part of our tour of Vietnam and whilst we were in Saigon. Admittedly we were unable to actually go inside the building as it is currently closed for major renovation work, however you can marvel at the architecture on the outside and also follow some of the billboards outside detailing some of the views of the interior. I can imagine that it will look absolutely splendid once finished in about 18 months time.

Explore and enjoy the French architecture influence of a bygone era. This Historic and prestigious heritage site, is built with brickworks from France, and is a former CIA residence with an interesting history. The sights of the city are very walkable. The famous Post Office sits just opposite the road, and the Independence Palace a short stroll through the park. The close proximity of all these tourist attractions make for an easy Walking tour. Other Centralised attractions such as the opera house and government house can also be accessed by foot.  TRAFFIC  There is a traffic/pedestrian controller/chaperone, who will actually walk with visitors trying to cross the crazy Vietnamese traffic. So basically his job is to cross the road all day, ensuring the safety of tourists and visitors to the Notre dame church square. This gesture is warm and appreciated, as the traffic can sometimes be frightful and hazardous. Both human and vehicle traffic can be busy.  Despite the renovations going on, the church is still spectacular. Unfortunately it was not open to visitors on our visit due to onsite construction/maintenance, however the post office next door will save the day! Both are magnificent and beautiful superstructures.
The Cathedral is basically of French design. It is very beautiful but unfortunately it is closed for renovation. Cannot enter. Maybe closed for 2 years

The church is built with red bricks and tiles and has a strong Asian style. Although it was not yet fully repaired and could not be visited when I went at the end of April, it is still very worthwhile to see.
A very beautiful and architecturally elaborate Catholic cathedral from the late 19th century. It seats 1,200 people and boasts an organ with 7,374 pipes and 110 stops. A must-see!

Its certainly worth visiting when in saigon. The architecture is really nice. Too bad its closed when i was there due to the church reconstuction or renovation (not really sure which of the two) but i bet the inside architecture looks great too.

Right next to the Central Post Office, how could I miss it? It's also a great spot for taking photos. It's in a European style, but I didn't go inside; it seemed to be under renovation, so I couldn't go in.
Next to the post office, the cathedral is another spot to visit in district 1. It is beautiful and gives you further insight into the history of Vietnam
I visited to Ho Chi Minh City. I chose Duc Ba church to visit first. It is very crowded. So, Duc Ba church is beautiful. I love it. I love Vietnam.
Don't miss their wonderful Sunday Mass in English at 9.30. Full house. Fans and huge AC units make it very comfortable

Now it is under contruction, and near the Center Post office with the French Artchitech and the Park, shopping plaza. It is convernient from here to go to Ben Thanh market. I love HCM city. But the traffic jam and crowned

A visit to this church which is very old and historical place, since it is under construction can visit only from outside

Sharing pictures of very popular Notre Dame Cathedral in Ho Chi Minh City ,situated in dist 1,opposite to Central Post office .this one of the key Tourist spots in dist 1.There is McDonald outlet as well very nearer to the cathedral which us open 24 hrs .there is also a street with book shops just opposite McDonald .war remnants museum too is near by .

This is a really beautiful cathedral. Just a disappointment, it was under renovation. Still can take pictures but there is a structure on both sides.  Anyway , it’s a place that u have to see next to the Post office.

Notre dame is must visit landmark if you go to Ho Chi Minh. But I went there while the building is being renovated. So yeah but still good to strolling around this building. Beside the Notre Dame is Central Post Office so you just need a walk.
This is currently closed for renovation. I think until mid April at least. Wished we could have seen inside. Pity.

We came here with each other, my wife and I did enjoy our trip and this place very much. Nice to take photoes and talk kwith people.

Beautiful church in the middle of the town in front of the Post office where may happen to meet lots of Vietnamese wearing traditional clothes . Close by is a small walking street with souvenirs and books as well Hard Rock Café Restaurant.

It is beautiful from the outside and well worth the visit as you will probably be visiting the post office opposite, another great architectural masterpiece.

A very pretty church, a small replica of Notre Dame de Paris! It's a shame it was under renovation and couldn't be visited!
They are restoring this church . You can still take pictures but there is some work being done. You can not enter
So many reasons to visit this, just to sit and admire the beautiful built cathedral is enough but to learn the different stories of the building is well worth the visit
Located in the heart of saigon. Ancient pass with the architecture of the French.This brings me a sense of peace and happiness.
A very beautiful cathedral, unfortunately for us, part of it is under renovation so the photos were somewhat spoiled.
Saigon's beautiful cathedral, built of red and pink brick. The interior is unremarkable but peaceful. A pleasant visit.
This palace is always beautiful, although it's currently undergoing restoration, surrounded by scaffolding. Taking interesting photos is impossible.
Opposite of Central Post Office. Very easy and near in center of town. Really interesting. Must visit !

It's quite a surprising building; once you sit down, you'll find a magnificent cathedral inside. Unfortunately, it's currently under renovation and surrounded by scaffolding. It's still worth seeing, though. It's located next to the post office, which is also magnificent.

This is the place you can't miss. Its not beautiful only oitside but inside make sure u make enough photos, and always be careful ofnu stuffs like phone wallet etc...

the cathedral was under renovation while we were there but it is simply breathtaking to see this old building, which is a copy of the Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris.

This church has impressive architecture and it is historical place in the city. Even it is closed for renovation, it is still worth a visit for the pictures.

Notre dame basilica easy to find at central town, so nice design . Great place for the photographer. The best time visit that during on sunday at 8-9am.

After many years trying to get original materials off the French they can finally start renovating this beautiful building. Unfortunately we visited on a Saturday and were unable to go inside, I believe midweek you can go inside.

Ho Chi Minh City Cathedral is home to many devout Catholics. While it used to be possible to enter the cathedral, tourists are now only allowed to view it from the entrance. Across the street is the Central Post Office, designed by the same person who designed the Eiffel Tower, making it a tourist spot within walking distance of hotels in the city. The surrounding area has undergone rapid modernization, and what was once one of the largest buildings in the city now stands alone, seemingly a relic of the past. Note that it is closed in the evenings and for Sunday mass. Also, please keep your voice down if there are people praying inside. The Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon is one of the most beautiful landmarks in Ho Chi Minh City — a peaceful escape in the middle of the city’s energy. Built with red bricks imported from France in the late 1800s, this stunning cathedral stands as a reminder of the city’s rich colonial past and architectural elegance. Even from afar, the twin bell towers and Romanesque design immediately draw your attention. Inside, the stained-glass windows filter soft light through intricate patterns, creating a calm and spiritual atmosphere. It’s not just a religious site — it’s a symbol of culture and history that both locals and travelers admire. Whether you’re into architecture, photography, or simply want a quiet spot to reflect, this cathedral is a must-visit in Saigon. Early morning visits are best, as the surrounding area is peaceful and perfect for photos. A timeless piece of history that beautifully connects Vietnam’s past and present.

The exterior is currently under renovation so there isn't much to see from the street. The staff were lovely, guiding visitors and giving info. It's a neat building to visit, built in the 1800s. Screens with responses and songs are easy to read. Lights are turned off pretty fast after service, get your photos quick!

Notre Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon is in the midst of a 10-year renovation and the only way to see the church is to attend Mass, which was quite crowded, especially with young people.

Designed by Jules Bourard, the church was built in 1877 in the Neo-Romanesque and Gothic styled with materials imported from France.

It is famous for its twin 180 foot bell towers, 68 foot dome, and red-brick façade, now under scaffolding. The church holds 1,200 people.

Notre Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon, a prominent historical landmark in Ho Chi Minh City, is a neo-Romanesque church built by French colonists between 1877 and 1883. It is known for its distinctive red brick facade and twin bell towers.
A statue of the Virgin Mary stands in the square in front of the cathedral, which is a popular spot for visitors. The building is situated in the city's government quarter, across from the Central Post Office.

The Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon, built between 1863 and 1880 by French colonists, is a key landmark in Ho Chi Minh City. The Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon is a mesmerizing testament to architectural brilliance and historical significance. Nestled in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City, this landmark seamlessly combines French colonial charm with local cultural influences. The cathedral's iconic twin towers soar into the sky, creating a striking silhouette against the urban backdrop. The elaborate facade, adorned with intricate details, reflects the meticulous craftsmanship of its construction. Stepping inside, visitors are immersed in a world of vibrant stained glass and serene interiors, evoking a sense of spiritual tranquility. Beyond its religious importance, the cathedral stands as a living relic, narrating tales of Vietnam's past. In the midst of the bustling city, the Notre Dame Cathedral emerges as a timeless symbol of beauty and heritage, beckoning travelers to uncover its rich tapestry.

The Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon is a historic 19th-century French-built landmark in Ho Chi Minh City's District 1, known for its red-brick facade and twin spires. Located in Paris Square, it remains a central Catholic hub in southern Vietnam. Note: The cathedral is currently undergoing major renovations with completion expected in 2027, though exterior viewing is still popular.

The Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon, officially known as the Basilica of Our Lady of The Immaculate Conception, is an iconic landmark in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. Built in the late 19th century by French colonists, it showcases stunning Romanesque architecture with its distinctive red bricks imported from France and twin bell towers. Inside, the peaceful atmosphere and intricately designed stained-glass windows offer a retreat from the bustling city. The cathedral is surrounded by a charming courtyard with a statue of the Virgin Mary, making it both a spiritual and cultural symbol of the city's colonial past and its enduring beauty.

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral, built in the late 1880s by French colonists, is one of the few remaining strongholds of Catholicism in the largely Buddhist Vietnam. Located in Paris Square, the name Notre Dame was given after the installation of the statue 'Peaceful Notre Dame' in 1959.
In 1962, the Vatican conferred the Cathedral status as a basilica and gave it the official name of Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica. Measuring almost 60 meters in height, the cathedral’s distinctive neo-Romanesque features include the all-red brick façade (which was imported from Marseille), stained glass windows, two bell towers containing six bronze bells that still ring today, and a peaceful garden set in the middle of downtown Ho Chi Minh City District 1.

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral officially began construction in 1877 and was completed in 1880. This building is considered one of the city's most iconic religious and architectural landmarks. Designed by the French architect J. Bourard, the cathedral was later supplemented with two pointed spires in 1895 according to a design by architect Gardès, raising its total height to 57 meters. At that time, it was the tallest building in Saigon.

Located in Paris Commune Square, the cathedral has no surrounding fence, creating an open and friendly atmosphere. The exterior is built of unplastered red brick, contributing to its enduring beauty after more than 140 years. Some of the bricks and tiles were produced in Saigon, combined with materials imported from France, giving it a unique blend of Eastern and Western styles.

The cathedral's facade features three large arched doorways, inspired by the Vietnamese three-arched gateway style. Above are inscriptions praising God and the Virgin Mary in Latin, French, and Chinese. The interior is 21 meters high, with a wide dome, rows of pews running along both sides, and over 50 stained glass windows depicting biblical stories.

In front of the church, there used to be a statue of Bishop Pigneau de Béhaine holding Prince Cảnh. After 1959, this statue was replaced with Our Lady of Peace, a familiar image every Christmas season. Notably, in 1962, the Vatican officially elevated the building to the status of a Basilica, recognizing the church's religious and architectural significance in the region.

Floor plan shows the church in the shape of a sprawling cross, over 91 meters long and nearly 36 meters wide. It is not only a center of Catholic activity but also a focal point for the community's spiritual connection.

"It can be said that the French-style architecture of Notre Dame Cathedral, together with the Central Post Office building, creates a charming Parisian corner of Saigon. To this day, the cathedral is not only a beautiful architectural structure but also a multicultural symbol of the city," researcher Tran Huu Phuc Tien wrote in his book.

The Basilica of Notre Dame Cathedral in Saigon, officially the Cathedral Basilica of Our Lady of the Immaculate Conception, is a cathedral located in the city of Saigon. The cathedral, founded by French administrators who initially called it the Church of Saigon, was built between 1877 and 1880. The name Notre-Dame Cathedral has been used since 1959, when a statue of Our Lady of Peace was placed in its vestibule. It has two bell towers, reaching a height of 58 meters.
Unfortunately, it is under very long restoration and is framed in ugly scaffolding. I was not able to go inside.

I visited in February when the church was under renovation, so I didn't have the chance to explore this iconic religious landmark of the city in detail. However, from the outside, the striking red Marseille bricks and the two tall bell towers were still noticeable.

In front of the church is the statue of Our Lady of Peace. Created by sculptor G. Ciocchetti in 1959, the statue is 4.6 meters tall, weighs 8 tons, and is made of white Italian marble. It was sculpted with the intention of being viewed from a distance, so it wasn't polished; therefore, the entire statue, including the face, still shows rough carving marks. The statue depicts Our Lady standing upright, holding a globe with a cross affixed to it. Her eyes gaze upwards as if praying for peace in Vietnam and for the world. The park surrounding the church also has many doves, a symbol of peace.

It's a shame that renovations are currently underway (August 2025), preventing us from seeing the basilica in all its magnificence.

A colossal building, erected in the late 19th century by the French colonial rulers in the then-fashionable Neo-Romanesque style...

...and which fell into disrepair after the occupiers withdrew in the 1950s.

Renovations have been underway for years, but at the time of our visit in April 2025, most of the complex was still shrouded in scaffolding and tarpaulins.

Nevertheless, a visit to Saigon wouldn't be complete without seeing this imposing building with its two 60-meter-high towers.

In some places, the magnificent red brickwork is still visible, allowing one to imagine the grandeur that once emanated from this structure. And how it might shine again someday. Perhaps as we experienced it on a previous visit (2015). See the photo from back then!

But the entire square on which the church stands is also well worth a visit. A number of interesting buildings stand here, and the main post office, built in 1891, is the most significant of them.

In the evenings, the square fills up with younger people. The atmosphere there is excellent and relaxed. Our hotel wasn't far away, and we thoroughly enjoyed the very friendly atmosphere there every evening.

The Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral (or Immaculate Conception Cathedral Basilica, French: Cathédrale Notre-Dame de Saïgon), often simply called Notre Dame Cathedral, is the cathedral of the Archdiocese of Saigon.

The cathedral is not only a symbol of Catholicism in Vietnam, but also one of the unique architectural works of Ho Chi Minh City and a popular tourist destination. Its original name was Saigon Cathedral (French: l'eglise de Saïgon), and the name Notre Dame Cathedral was adopted in 1959 with the placement of the Statue of Our Lady of Peace in front of the grounds.

The cathedral is the site of the ordination of many bishops, the reception of representatives from the Holy See in Rome, the inauguration of archbishops, and the ordination of thousands of priests.

Notre-Dame Cathedral is one of the most symbolic structures of French colonial heritage in Ho Chi Minh City. Completed in 1880, the red bricks used in its construction were imported directly from Marseille. Architecturally, it reflects the French Gothic style; it has a simple yet majestic presence. Its two bell towers on the facade are immediately noticeable in the city skyline.

However, unfortunately, the interior is currently closed to visitors due to renovation work. It is possible to see and photograph the exterior, but some of that impressive atmosphere is missing.

For me, while the building is impressive in terms of its architectural history, the square and surrounding area felt a bit too touristy and crowded. Although it doesn't create a truly 'trans-inducing' atmosphere, it is still one of the places to see for those who want to feel the French traces in the city. I give it four stars, maybe five after the restoration.

⛪ Notre Dame Cathedral in Saigon – A historical and cultural icon in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City

Architecture: An exquisite Roman-Gothic structure, built entirely of imported French red brick, notable for its two 60-meter-high bell towers and vibrant stained-glass windows.

History: Inaugurated in 1880, the interior space is solemn with high domes and a sturdy load-bearing structure.

Grounds: In front is a marble statue of Our Lady of Peace, an ideal photo spot.

Experience: Located in the bustling District 1, this is both a religious destination and a tourist attraction, offering a peaceful and sacred feeling.

Preservation: After more than 140 years, the cathedral still retains its ancient charm, clean, and free from moss and mold thanks to its special exposed brickwork.

In summary: Notre Dame Cathedral is an ideal destination for sightseeing, learning about history, and appreciating its unique architecture, combining cultural and religious experiences in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City.

- Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral (officially: Immaculate Conception Cathedral Basilica, French: Cathédrale Notre-Dame de Saïgon, abbreviated as Notre Dame Cathedral) is the cathedral of the Archdiocese of Ho Chi Minh City.

Notre Dame Cathedral of Saigon is Saigon's most famous landmark. The Central Post Office is located to its left. Originally named Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral, it is also known as the Red Cathedral. All the red bricks used in its construction were imported from France, and after more than a century, their color remains vibrant and unfaded. Modeled after the bell towers of Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris, its symmetrical and majestic design features two towers reaching forty meters in height. In front of the main hall stands a four-ton statue of the Virgin Mary, a gift from the Roman Catholic Church in 1945. Address: 01 Công xã Paris, Bến Nghé, Quận 1, Hồ Chí Minh 70000 Vietnam. Transportation: Bus No. 36. Admission: Free. Time: Unknown.

i visited Notre Dame cathedral in 2017, that time already under renovation, but only the back of the building, still can see the facade of the building, after few years, when i had my 9 days trip to HCM city in 2023,i came here again, it is still under renovation, but this time, cant see the facade of it, only the back can be seen, and also cant go inside the church. so i just took some pics of the St.Mary statute oustide the church.it is erected in front of the building,solemn and merciful. will come here again,hope that time ,can see the whole building and also can go inside the church.

The cathedral, built between 1863 and 1880, has been under renovation for several years. It is the cathedral of the Archdiocese of Saigon, where a significant Catholic minority lives. The interior, which is designed to accommodate around 1,200 believers, cannot currently be visited; the work is scheduled to be completed in 2027. This is probably also because the building materials for the cathedral are being sourced from Europe.

Duc Ba Cathedral in Saigon is a true architectural marvel! The stunning design and rich history make it a must-visit. The intricate details and serene atmosphere create a memorable experience. Highly recommended for those exploring Saigon's cultural gems.

We have been to HCMC many times and have visited this place on several occasions though not every time. It is one of the key landmarks in District 1. Ho Chi Minh Cathedral, also known as Notre Dame Cathedral, is a symbol of Ho Chi Minh City, characterized by its two towers rising behind a statue of the Virgin Mary built during the French colonial era. Located in Ho Chi Minh City, it is a very distinctive red brick church. We visited in January 2023, but unfortunately the church was under renovation and we could not enter. The exterior walls were also covered with steel scaffolding for repairs.

I will have to make do with photos off the internet !

Loved visiting the cathedral on a Sunday morning for mass, it is at 9:30 for English mass. This was the only way to visit the cathedral as it is closed for renovations and nothing is visible from outside.

If we expect to find a Notre Dame Cathedral in Ho Chi Minh City, we're simply mistaken. But it is undeniably valuable. Very nice architecture walking around this building on the outside, shame we couldn’t go inside due to refurbishment work ongoing inside. Great photo opportunity anyway from outside, just across Road from Post Office so combining the two makes sense.
SUperb architecture and was lovely to see on a lovely sunny day however disappointing we couldn't go inside due to renovation works taking place. Nice to see thigh.

This cathedral is near the post office and Nguyen Hue. I recommend to see it as well as crossing the road to visit the post office.  It has a very original architecture given that it is in Vietnam. The Cathedral has a prominent location in downtown Ho Chi Minh City; however it was closed when we stopped by on our group walking tour. As Ho Chi Minh City was formerly the capital of French Indochina, many colonial-era buildings remain. The most representative of these is Notre Dame Cathedral, which serves as the city's landmark. Constructed with red bricks imported from France, it features spires approximately 40 meters high standing on both sides, with a statue of the Virgin Mary at the front. Currently, Notre Dame Cathedral is under renovation. When taking photos, you must step much further forward than the statue of the Virgin Mary to ensure the spires fit into the frame.

Notre Dame Cathedral is one of Ho Chi Minh City's most important landmarks. This Catholic church is just one street away from the Saigon Post Office. Modeled after the bell towers of Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris, the cathedral boasts a symmetrical and majestic design, with two towers reaching forty meters into the sky. The exterior walls are constructed of red bricks transported from Marseille in southern France, and after a century, they remain vibrant red, hence its nickname, the Red Church. While visiting, consider attending a Mass to experience the atmosphere. In front of the church stands a four-ton statue of the Virgin Mary. The exterior portico and other areas are adorned with exquisite carvings, while the interior features numerous small chapels, each with its own unique shrines, sculptures, and decorations, creating a harmonious blend of beauty and variation. Admission: Free. Opening Hours: 8:00-11:00, 15:00-16:00 daily. Mass times: Weekdays 5:30, 17:00; Sundays 5:30, 6:30, 9:30, 16:00, 17:15, 18:30. Entry is permitted outside of Mass times, but the building closes for a midday rest. Recommended visit time: 0.5-1 hour.
