Its a small replica of the Notre Dame. I saw it when passing by. It is usually touristy and you will many people around even late at night.
Nice exterior of Notre dame cathedral but unable to access interior as under renovation until june 2020
Located in Ho Chi Mihn City, built in 1863, it looked great from the outside.. but unfortunately we were not able to go inside.

A very beautiful building, dating from the French colonial period. A visit is recommended in conjunction with a visit to the post office and town hall. Currently closed to visitors due to renovations.
It was sunny day but my couple enjoyed to take a lot of photo.  It was such good experience to visit here.
This is next to post office and was under construction so we couldn’t go inside Looked at it from outside -

This large red brick church, built during the French colonial era, is located at the end of Dong Khoi Street, heading north. A statue of the Virgin Mary stands at the front. As of July 2019, the sides and back of the church were covered with scaffolding due to ongoing repair work.

I went there as part of the standard route after visiting the Reunification Palace. I think it was about an 8-minute walk from the Reunification Palace. As expected of a major tourist spot, it was a very impressive church, but unfortunately, it was under renovation and I couldn't go inside. There were lots of people in the square, and there was a convenience store nearby, so it was quite lively.

Built on the same classic lines of other Notre Dame cathedrals elsewhere. Unluckily, the church itself was undergoing restoration, so was unable to enter and take photos from the inside of the stained glass windows. There is a lovely statue of Mother Mary outside the cathedral itself. facing the cathedral, on the right side is the famous Central Post office.

I came here on a Saturday morning. Sitting on the sidewalk, sipping coffee and admiring the ancient, majestic beauty of the church, I felt a sense of peace. Although it was closed for maintenance, many people still came to take photos as souvenirs. In the midst of the noisy and bustling Saigon, this is definitely a place worth visiting for tranquility.

Notre Dame Cathedral is a landmark of Ho Chi Minh City, and a visit to this beautiful church is a must when in the city. Next to the cathedral is the Central Post Office, another landmark where you can buy postcards and send them to friends.
I didn't go in. It had gotten very late that day, they were restoring it, but it looked very beautiful and had imposing architecture.
The tour bus had no places to stop, so we were delayed in getting to our destination. Even on a tour, I recommend using a motorbike taxi for more efficient travel (bringing your own helmet is best).

Great cathedral in the middle of the city center. Fantastic, just a shame you couldn't go inside. Our guide told us it had been closed for the last two years, but it was still worth the trip - we got to take some great pictures. The Post Office is also right across the street, so if you're on a city tour you'll get to see more things. The Opera House is also a couple of blocks away.

The Saigon Norte Dame Cathedral is a beautiful building with stunning architecture, however the building is currently undergoing restoration works, which restricts access to the building and limits some of the external architecture. However, still a stunning example of French Colonial Architecture withing Ho Chi Minh.
We were only able to view the Cathedral from the outside which was beautiful.  Apparently the building is under restoration.  The surrounding area is worth exploring especially the post office.

Last Saturday we toOk a walk from our hotel to visit the cathedral of Notre Dame. we found we could not get in till the next mass at 17:30. So e took a walk through the park nearby, saw the squirrels on the trees eating from plastic bags attached to the tree trunk with fruit inside. Further on, we came to the Palace of reconciliation. We walked back to the cathedral and attended Mass. Inside it is not very picturesque as with many other cathedrals. It was built in 1877 using stone/bricks imported from France. The choir was magnificent and just to hear them sing during the Mass, was worth the visit!

The Notre Dame Cathedral is a replica of the Paris Notre Dame. It even has a rose window. However when we visited, it was undergoing maintenance and had scaffolding around it, so we were unable to get too close to it or to Exeter it. It would be worth a visit however.

This landmark building in Ho Chi Minh City is conveniently located in the city center, but unfortunately it is closed for visits due to renovations. However, this does not diminish its unique charm. It is highly recommended to bring plenty of sun protection and drinking water.

For those interested in European architecture and Catholic architecture, this building will be of interest. Located in a spacious square in the city center, its beautiful colors immediately draw attention to its resemblance to Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris, despite the differences in size and colors. If you are visiting the city center and the Opera House, a visit to this building is highly recommended.

In addition to special experiences like tunnel tours and night views in Ho Chi Minh City, Notre Dame Cathedral is a must-visit city tour destination. Located in the city center, you can explore the area, including the Central Post Office, in just half a day by coordinating your itinerary with other attractions.
You can't miss it, it's very centrally located, opposite the post office. We couldn't go inside, it's under restoration... We only saw it from the outside, but it's beautiful.
Walking by on the way to the post-office so only saw it on the outside. Looking very nice. Some restauration work going on.
Couldn’t go in they were doing construction! Did go in the last time we were in Vietnam and it’s beautiful a must see!
Unfortunately, the back half of the cathedral was under construction and covered in scaffolding. However, this cathedral had an atmosphere unlike anything I've seen in Asia.
This is an impressive cathedral, although at the time of writing it’s undergoing renovation. If you’re close by it’s certainly worth having a look at, and there is plenty to see close by.
Very busy with foreign TV crews. It was the day before the USA-Korea summit. Nice and good restaurants around. Quite expensive though.

It was under construction, so I couldn't go inside. It was next to the post office. At first glance, it stands out because it looks so different from the surrounding buildings. It is undoubtedly a beautiful building. While I was idly looking at it, a group of Vietnamese middle school students, perhaps on a school trip or as part of a school lesson, asked me if they could ask me some questions in English. It was similar to the school activities in Kyoto where elementary and middle school students ask questions to foreigners in English. They asked me things like, "Where are you from? What did you come to see? How many days are you staying?" and it was quite heartwarming. Finally, they took a commemorative photo and disappeared.
This is a very nice history church located at district 1 of Ho Chi Minh city. It is just a few minute from my lovely school Lasan Tabert (the name before 1975) that is located at 53 Nguyen Du street.

The exact duration of the renovations is unknown; they've already been under repair for three years. Although it's open to visitors, the scaffolding still remains on the exterior walls, which is a bit of a shame, but it's still a must-see for photos.
Though I didn’t linger on a very hot day due to the renovations, I saw enough to get the impression that it was beautiful inside. Hope it will be better when finished!
I came here in a rainny day . Very beautifully ancient church , around have a lot of thing to try . I will come back

We took a 4 hour city tour in a private car with a guide and visited the Cathedral, Post Office and generally saw the french influence on architecture . Also visited the central market and emperor Jade Pagoda then the war remnants museum. A most enlightening and informative day thanks to our Vietnamese guide "Tin Tin"

We started out to see the Notre Dame Cathedral but upon arrival we saw lots of scaffold, as it was under renovation. For what we saw from the outside, it was impressive. However we managed to be interviewed by final year college students who asked foreign tourists in the forecourt about their journey to Vietnam. This was great as we were able to interact with the locals and learn something more about life in Vietnam.

Very beautiful facade. Inside the church (only allowed if you are Catholic and attending mass) it's quite old but it will be beautiful and grand after the renovation as the interior decor is similar to the European churches. Nice high ceilings, pillars etc.
This famous Saigon landmark is under much needed renovation. So we can only view it from the outside..  Next visit here.

I managed to attend a mass in this cathedral. It's truly very moving, listening to the choir, looking at the stained glass windows, and observing people of different ethnicities within a sacred place with a single purpose. I loved it.
Beautiful from the outside, but currently closed for visits due to renovations. The post office is conveniently located next door, making it easy to combine the two.

Although we couldn't go inside because it was under construction, it was a lovely church with a charming brick building. A beautiful statue of the Virgin Mary stood at the front, and it was bustling with tourists taking commemorative photos.

It’s pity that the Cathedral is on maintaining. So that I am not able to get into the Cathedral and see the whole picture of this great historical site. I would recommend you to visit here while you trip to MCH. BTW, a book street is nearby and it is also worthy to visit.

Visitation inside is restricted to worship. An the outside is shrouded in construction beams. It wasn't a time to enjoy the beauty of this edifice. The garden was being mulched. So, the only entertainment was the birds in odd colors.
We visited the Saigon Notre Dame easier this week on a tour. The building & gardens are lovely, however we were unable to go inside. Very nice for photographs from the outside.
Unfortunately the Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral is being renovated so you can go inside. But it looks like a magnificent cathedral from the outside.

It’s nice from the outside with a lovely garden and statue out in front.  We weren’t able to visit inside due to ongoing works.  Located next to the old post office which is also great to see.  Maybe next time we can visit inside.

Lovely building, next to several tourist sites so can see a bit of old HCM architecture ( read French colonial architecture) around same areas within 5 minutes walk. Nothing extraordinary but worth a quick look see.
It's attraction place for visiting when you're in Ho Chi Mint city. Unfortunately it was under construction when I visited.
Appeared closed for maintenance on our visit. Beautiful French Architecture and opposite the post office. Worth a visit.
Unfortunately, viewing was only possible from the outside. Very beautiful architecture. Renovation work is currently underway.
This is a beautiful building, but were not able to go inside as it looked closed for maintenance work. It is in a really cool part of the city too, with coffee shops and plenty of tourists.
Worth the trip to see, unfortunately it was closed off when we went but the architecture of the notre dame of Asia is reason enough to go. Next to the post office, well placed for two trips in one.
Notre Dame Cathedral was very beautiful, even despite the construction work. While visiting, you should also take a look at the post office.

Located in the centre of Paris square, this attractive cathedral is an important attraction of Saigon. The architecture outside and inside has been tastefully done. The statue of virgin Mary outside the cathedral is another great attraction
We came at the wrong time..it was under renovation and closed. But had a quick glimpse from the outside.

Notre Dame cathedral is one of those landmarks that is a remnant of Vietnam’s colonial past. The cathedral is currently undergoing restoration works which preclude you from entering the building. You are however able to view it perfectly well from every angle if you like to look at the detail of the architecture. The opera house and other excellent examples of French colonial architecture are located within five minutes walk of the cathedral.

One of Ho Chi Minh City's most iconic buildings is the Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral. Also known as the Church of Our Lady, this church is located at the northern end of Dong Khoi Street. When viewed from the front (facing Notre Dame Square), the red brick building is striking with its two pointed towers on either side. The back of the building was covered in scaffolding, indicating that extensive renovation work was underway, and it was not possible to tour the interior.
It happenes to be closed for renovations but you can get photos from the front. Nice gardens outside
It's a shame we got there during restoration work... We weren't able to see the inside... The exterior is impressive. Grandiose!

A centuries-old cathedral, definitely worth a visit. The view is stunning. It's a shame we arrived during restoration, as access was inaccessible, and some of the sides were covered in scaffolding. All in all, another must-see for anyone who enjoys exploring historic sites.

No we couldn't go in due to the renovations , but the outside was great, the garden out the front was a picture, and being tended for by gardeners. the views from the area were great, acrooss to the Post Office, over to the old CIA building where the last helicopter left from and down the tree lined street opposite that looks so french .

I stopped by while strolling around the city to take some photos. It was very crowded with tourists. Afterwards, you can mail postcards from the Saigon Post Office across the street. It's the kind of spot you can visit to help with digestion after a delicious abalone meal. 😌
I went there in March, but I could only look at it from the outside. It seems that construction is ongoing until May, so it's not possible to go inside.
It is good to see this at around sunset - that way you see it in daylight and as the night comes. Entry to the church is allowed if the main purpose is to pray...

Heard a lot about it and a MUST-DO when you come to Ho Chi Minh City. As expected, many tourists (maybe even locals) gathered to capture their best shot with the right angle and light. Slight disappointment was that the Cathedral was under some renovation works so we could not explore the interior, which i am sure would be a beauty! Don't forget to drop by the Post Office which is just next to it separated by a road.

Considering its age, the cathedral is in great shape. The exterior is being renovated right now. There is an English-language mass on Sundays at 9:30am. If the guard stops you at the gate, tell him you’re there to attend the mass and he will let you in. Otherwise, you’ll hear, “no tourists”. There’s a woman selling candles outside the gate. Cheap but as always you should negotiate :-)

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral, also known as the Church of Our Lady, has a courtyard called Notre Dame Square in front of it. The street extending from this square towards the Saigon River is Dong Khoi Street. Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral is characterized by its red brick construction and two symmetrical towers, one on the right and one on the left, when viewed from the front of the building (facing Notre Dame Square). The back of the building was covered with construction sheets, and since construction was underway, it was not possible to see inside.
A haven of peace in a manic area. Still used by the community and still a lovely building. Well worth a visit.
Would recommend anyone in town to stop by for a show if possible. It's an experience. I didn't understand much of the show but they were very good and fun to watch

It's unusual to see such things in Asia, when I'm used to Buddhist temples. The cathedral is beautiful, but is currently under renovation. According to legend (our guide), it's almost an exact replica of the Cathedral in Paris. This isn't true. The structures are similar (the materials for construction were brought to Saigon from France), but not identical. It's a highly visited landmark, but definitely worth a visit.
Very nice church and many bridal couples there to take wedding photos. Good location and shoot. Weather very hit didn't stay too long. Took photos and left. It's next to the post office building.

The Cathedral was on top of our list to visit while in Ho Chi Minh. Unfortunately, it was under renovation at the time of our visit. It was however, quite beautiful and very imposing from the outside. The architecture is very impressive. I’m an absolutely disappointed we didn’t get to see it inside.  In addition, the just as impressive Post Office is across the road.
A long history, it is well worth a visit even by non catholics. It has endured many transitions and survived
The Notre Dame Cathedral is under renovation at the moment so could only view from behind a barrier. A very imposing building and them outside looks beautiful
Quite a dominant building and worth a quick look around. Close to many other attractions, so convenient to see. The post office is also just across the road.

Definitely worth a visit if you're in the area. Not everyday you see the Notre Dame in Asia. Especially as it is close to the pretty post office and busy area with restaurants/shopping.  However, do note that it is currently under refurbishment (March 2019) and it does not look near to finishing yet. You can still see it is the Notre Dame though and take pictures from the front.
This can be done for its convenience next to the post office and near the imperial palace. The cathedral is beautiful but was unforetunately under contruction.

The Notre Dame Church certainly makes a fine impression in the city of Saigon, though it doesn't quite compare to the stunning creations of the Old World. Built in France, this building clearly demonstrates the French's desire to leave a lasting impression on this country. Open only from 8:00 to 11:00, it's recommended to also visit the main post office in the same square, designed by the famous Gustave Eiffel.
I visited this church under maintenance and very crowded on Sunday. But I thought this construction is really an attractive site. Good to enjoy lunch at MC Donald nearby.
We passed by here in the evening, and all around us were locals sitting on small chairs, buying food and drinks from street vendors and eating... Great lifestyle here.

It's a very beautiful building in one of the most beautiful parts of the city. We can admire the facade and its two lovely bell towers, but the restoration work prevents us from seeing the interior. That will have to wait for another time.
Opposite the post office this old cathedral is worth a visit. The building is clean and attractive and a great photo opportunity.
although it was under construction when I visited. Despite that, there were lots of people there, tourists and locals. It seems to be how Saigon is, normally.

It was a Sunday... During a religious service. A magnificent, unforgettable moment, thanks to the devotion of the congregation and the singing. Extremely lively and dynamic. Nothing comparable to our churches. A wonderful moment.
A classical church, topped with two square bell towers and 40-meter-high spires visible from all the city's high terraces. If you go in, you won't leave until the end of Mass!
Sadly the cathedral is closed for renovation so we could only admire it from the outside. It is a very beautiful Cathedral located next to the Post Office. Not sure when it reopens.

The building is far from its Parisian or Montreal brothers in appearance. It looks impressive, but its shell is damaged and it is noticeable that the reconstruction can be done on the walls, which is also not in dispute. Interested parties should be allowed in so that they can see one of the Asian masterpieces of Christian culture! So that they can see it and pray... among other things, so that the reconstruction can be completed as soon as possible in the house of God.

We were hesitant to visit the Cathedral as we read it was under renovation. However, we took a chance and attended the 9:30 English Sunday mass, there is a guard who tell tourists that they are closed, we were not stopped and was able to go into church for the mass.  It is a huge, solemn and beautiful church, definitely worth stopping by for mass.

At the end of 2018, Ho Chi Minh City was undergoing a lot of construction, and this church was also under construction. It's a fairly large and impressive church. The Central Post Office is located on the same square, so it's a place visited by many tourists.
Although the cathedral was unfortunately closed during our visit, it is still a real eye-catcher from the outside.
We were fortunate enough to be here on a Sunday for Mass and were permitted to go in as not all locals are allowed in just to have a look.
It looks beautiful from the main street. Since it was during Tet (Vietnamese New Year), there were large decorations. The square in front of the church was well-maintained and beautiful.
The outside is very gorgeous with nice architecture. Unfortunately, it is being close for renovation. Look forward to seeing the whole when it's done.
This could've been a very pretty cathedral but it's under reconstruction so it's impossible to get in.
Even for someone not religious, this is great to see to admire the architecture. It truly is postcard perfect.
dating back to very old times this church is undergoing some renovation to restore the original shape. stones are getting imported. still looking majestic.

Because the opening hours are short, I was finally able to get inside on my third visit. It's quite difficult to visit if you're sightseeing in other areas, so I recommend planning your visit carefully.

This cathedral is very beautiful, but unfortunately, the sides are hidden by scaffolding because it's under construction. Furthermore, it's impossible to visit it; it's only open to worshippers attending Mass. It's a shame because we didn't have time to go to one of the daily services.
We were only able to see the outside because it was undergoing restoration. The guide explained that the interior was quite bare.
Beautiful from the outside. The architecture is amazing and breathtaking. However it is currently closed (with no sign of when it will reopen). Still it is worth a visit.
We seemed to walk for ages to reach this. It is a popular place, but I wouldn't bother. Personally we would spend our time sat having a drink people watching.

Only reason the cathedral gets 4 "stars" is because it was closed for renovation during my visit in December 2018. Fortunately, they had poster size photos of the interiors on fencing surrounding the building. The exterior of the church is really beautiful and they had a very nice Nativity set in front of the church for the Christmas season. I look forward to returning one day to see the fully renovated cathedral! Worth your time when visiting HCM city.
This lovely old cathedral is opposite the old post office . Although at the moment there is scaffolding all the way around the building .
The style of the Parisian church of Notre Dame. It's a shame that construction was underway and we couldn't see the inside. From the outside, it's a beautiful structure.
The cathedral is beautiful from what I could tell. Unfortunately during our visit it was partially obstructed by scaffolding due to some restoration work.

This is a really nice church and presents a great photo opportunity as it does look like Notre Dame!  It is right next to the old Post Office so worth doing together!  However when we went, there was a function so we were not able to go inside. Therefore cannot comment on the interior!
This is one of the places I wanted to visit. Unfortunately, there's a time lag, making it difficult to get inside. I'm not sure when it will be finished.
This cathedral is very impressive just opposite the post office, there was some repair work happening and a very busy tourist spot, still very impressive having been built in the early 1800s

As of January 19, 2019, it was under construction, so I was unable to go inside. It was nice to be able to see it from a distance, but it was still a shame not to be able to see the interior. However, the building is beautiful enough just from the exterior alone. It is located right in front of the Central Post Office and is not far from the Reunification Palace, making it a convenient location to combine sightseeing with the palace. I hope that the construction will be finished by the time I visit Ho Chi Minh City again so that I can see the inside.
It is a very beautiful cathedral basilica that we could not fully enjoy because it was under construction.
Didn't go inside but was pretty from the outside! Was some construction at the side and back of the building but still managed to get a nice picture.

Unfortunately, the cathedral was undergoing renovations and closed to the public. The exterior façade was covered in scaffolding. However, we were still able to see the main façade, which was beautiful.
It was closed the day that we went so I can't comment on the interior. Gorgeous flower garden in front.
Nice Church for a few holiday pictures, i didnt go inside but the outside was beautiful, to take some photos.
The cathedral with its red bricks from France is simply magnificent, a must-see, it's a shame we can't visit the inside.

It's worth enduring 40 minutes of mass in Vietnamese, with a choir and the priest singing almost the entire time. It's very beautiful. You have to say you're a practicing Christian and that you want to see it because they don't allow tourists in; they only let you in if you're going to stay for the whole mass.
A very beautiful cathedral in the French style, where two services are held daily and which can only be visited then!

The cathedral is a beautiful building. I was unable to go inside and they're currently doing construction yo the outside. It was a significant amount of history. If you visit the post office it's directly across the street.
Unfortunately, the church is currently being renovated and cannot be entered. However, it's still worth a stop. There's a rather nice view from the front.

We could not enter the Cathedral during the day. However there is still a daily mass that is conducted in the afternoon. Suggest to attend the mass if you want to see the Cathedral from the inside. The service is conducted in Vietnamese.
This place was closed for renovation but from the outside has a lot of great history behind it. I'm certain the inside would have been great. Picture worthy.
I've attended Mass here a few times. The priest's sermons are excellent, inspiring, and very heartwarming. I'm overwhelmed by the vast space with its sacred atmosphere.

The Notre Dame Cathedral looked so beautiful in the photos I saw that it was one of the tourist attractions I was looking forward to the most. Unfortunately, however, construction was currently underway.
We didn’t go into the cathedral but took some photos. It is very grand. If we had more time we would have liked to have looked inside.
Built in the 1880s by French colonists. It is one of the remaining strongholds of Catholicism just opposite the Central Post office. it was under renovation could view from outside

There are so many beautiful pagodas to see in Vietnam so there’s not a lot of attention to Cathedrals but this is a great spot to visit in HCMC. One star off bc it was undergoing construction while I was there and we couldn’t access the inside but definitely worth a visit and your inside if you can.

This church is located at the end of Dong Khoi Street. Many people gathered around it day and night. Unfortunately, the church was under construction and I couldn't see it, but the church surrounded by its framework was a unique sight that I couldn't see until then, and it was better than I expected.

This church was built in the likeness of Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris, but it is currently under construction, so access is restricted to general visitors other than those attending Mass. Taking a photo in front of the church makes for a beautiful shot.

I couldn't go inside, so I only took pictures of the exterior. As expected of a tourist spot, there were people from various countries. It was a shame that it started raining halfway through, but I think it would be a wonderful place on a sunny day.
Very pretty, but it was under renovation when I visited. A shame :-) It's worth a visit. Go for a stroll in this neighborhood. In the heart of District 1.
Currently undergoing renovations, so visits inside are not possible. A shame. Close to the post office.

Renovations were on when we visited in Nov 2018. From a distance looks like a lovely place. Dont know how long it will take but there are other places to visit in the same area so your trip to this will not go waste.
It is beautiful art and craft, just bad, the exterial was covered by project partition. Recommended to attend the service, will realy enjoy the interior design
Notre dame is the cathedral of Saigon. English mass is at 9:30 on Sunday. The whole complex is under going renovations at the moment and the church is open only for masses on Sunday.
Well worth a visit, especially to take a photo of the exterior. Very impressive inside too if there is a service happening.
Inside access was closed when we walked by ;however, we were easily able to circle and photograph this beautiful historic church!

Unfortunately, Notre Dame Cathedral was covered in scaffolding on the sides. Nevertheless, it looked beautiful from the front, from the park. Vietnamese vendors were also trying to sell trinkets there.
The place now under renovation so, you only can go to take a photo from the outside of the church. Worth place to go since just cross the road to Old post office.
realy nice architecture, from outside we couldn't go inside they had an event, but the outside looks great

I was not able to go inside as the Cathedral was closed. However, a quick stop here is one of the givens the most tourist will have when visiting Ho Chi Minh City. Its exterior garden and especially the divine statue of the Virgin gazing skyward are marvels and very photogenic in the midst of an otherwise very chaotic street scene. Look both ways and proceed at risk of your life when crossing the road in the vicinity of this landmark. It is located near the other chief architectural attraction downtown - the renovated Post Office.

It was a magnificent view going there to take pictures but sadly it's under construction now so you won't be able to go inside and the ground level wouldn't be as aesthetic as usual. Overall, it's still a good place to visit.
It is adjacent to the Saigon Central Post Office. It was popularly known as Saigon Church.It is currently closed for renovation.

This Roman Catholic cathedral was completed and concentrated in 1880. Most of the cathedral’s beautiful exterior architecture is visible, but it is closed for a major renovation. It two sides are covered with scaffolding.
It is very nice from the outside. Unfortunately closed for renovations at the moment so we could not go in. It's next to the post office.
Beautiful old church. An icon to see in HCMC surrounded by the buzz of the city. Go see it, go see the Post Office building next to it then go sit in one of the coffee shots there to people watch.

The title says it all. Unfortunately, during my trip from December 8th to 20th, 2018, the cathedral was under renovation, so it was impossible to visit. That said, architecturally, the building is very beautiful. I hope to be able to see the interior one day.

Located next to Saigon Central Post Office, this Norte Dame Cathedral is not to be missed when go to HCMC. Went there on 2-Dec-2018 around noon, this Cathedral was under renovation. Didn’t get to see the entire building. Weather was super hot! Not too many tourist outside. And the pictures turn out beautiful with blue sky!
The church can currently only be admired from the outside, as it is closed for renovations. A pity for us, but great for future generations!
Built in 1880, unfortunately could not go inside, but still nice to marvel at from out front, nice statue of Mary too.

This is one of the colonial buildings in Ho Chi Minh City. It's directly across from the Central Post Office, and the outside was bustling with tourists taking pictures. There were especially many people near the entrance, and it was impossible to go inside anyway, so I focused on taking commemorative photos from a little further away. Leaving something undone will lead to a next time. Until next time.

When I visited, it was under construction and I couldn't go inside, but it was overwhelmingly large. The weather was nice, so the colors stood out against the blue sky. You have to cross a pedestrian crossing without traffic lights, so you might not be able to get across unless you stick close to other people.
Unfortunately, we could only view the church from the outside, as it was undergoing renovations. Nevertheless, it's a beautiful church, located next to the old main post office.

Cathedral signing through renovation at present, so part of it is covered in scaffolding. Luckily for us it is not covering the entrance, not stopping the celebration of Mass either.  It is the first time that I have gone to a Mass in a foreign language, other than Latin. We were still able to keep up though.  The inside of the building is beautiful, without being over the top.

A classic Gothic cathedral set majestically in its own traffic island. Although only open for limited hours, if you can get inside it is worthwhile - a peaceful relief from the hustle and bustle outside. Of special interest is the statue of the Virgin with a blinking neon halo!
Lovely place to visit but make sure to wear modest clothing in order to be allowed in. The outside is beautiful, definately worth a few photographs! The architecture is great.
Nice for photos. Cross the street with the post office ( a tourist spot). There some maintenance work going on so only photo outside ( Nov 2018)
Since it is adjacent to the old-fashioned post office, both buildings look beautiful. It was a bit of a shame that I couldn't go inside because it was under construction.

The cathedral is under renovation. It is not open when we visit it (on Saturday). Hence we can only look at it from external.  The exterior of the cathedral is absolutely beautiful. It is not possible to miss this building. I do wish to visit again to see its interior.

On my second visit, the Central Post Office is located right next door, making it convenient to visit both together. It is a great location for taking a leisurely stroll, looking around, and sitting down in a nearby park to rest if you get tired.

It's one of the most striking buildings in Ho Chi Minh City. It was under construction, so I couldn't go inside, but along with the post office, it has an appearance unlike anything you'd expect in Southeast Asia. It's relatively new.

It's currently under renovation and not open for visits, but just taking photos outside is enough to make you happy. The soft red color evokes a sense of bliss, and everyone taking pictures in front of the church has a sweet smile on their face.

Currently undergoing renovations, there are restrictions on entering the cathedral . With the construction shields around the cathedral it is difficult to marvel the old French architecture or get good photos. However you can still see a large number of tourists outside taking snaps in front of the cathedral.  Skip if you do not have the time - at least until they open after renovations

The whole cathedral was surrounded by construction shields and so there wasn't much to see. I also didn't see an entrance to get in? There were mass timings posted, but these didn't work out for me. So, fellow travelers, please check.
Although I couldn't go inside... it was a pink church! The color was such a gentle pink that just looking at it made me feel warm inside.
It was a bit bigger than I expected it to be but, as beautiful as I was expecting it. Sadly it was under some kind of maintenance so I didn’t get to see the inside.

There is an English language mass on Sundays at 9:30. Frequently with choral music. It is crowded, but definitely worth the visit. This is a relic of the French occupation ane one of the better tings the French left behind. Across the square is the Post Office. Not to be missed either.

This is on of the reasons why i come to vietnam. I want to visit saigon notredame cathedral.  It was a great building. With European architecture. And it more special with a virgin marry statua right in front of the cathedral.  They said, the statue dropped some tears in year 2004. And it became famous and popular. Many people from other city in vietnam came to ho chi min to see if the statue.  Anyway It was beautiful landmark. Unfortunately it under reconstruction. So we can't come in.

In French time it is founded this church its name Notre Dame Cathedral but it is not replica of the original one in Paris. When we arrived we found out that it is under restıration and maintenance. That is why we couldn’t have chance to see inside.
The cathedral is located opposite the post office. It's very pretty from the outside. But there's major renovation work underway. It's impossible to get close to it, let alone go inside!
Based on the model of Notre Dame de Paris, but smaller, this cathedral is truly a very beautiful work, currently under renovation.
Combined with the Post Office, which is just the other side of the road, it’s quite a sight. Despite major work being carried out when we visited it’s truly beautiful piece of architecture
Location near Central Post Office and Book Street. This historic building is visited by many tourists because of its beautiful architecture. Suitable as a photo location.
Here we are in front of this lovely Catholic cathedral made of red brick. Currently undergoing restoration, it is closed to visitors. What a shame!

Ho Chi Minh City A place to people watch!  Visit Ho Chi Minh In late October Day Temperature around 30c stayed in district 1 which was great for museum and art galleries all a little walk away. Some of the points of interest were the old post office in the square,with the Cathedral, both are of old French architecture. Go inside the post office It is FREE, look out for the old man who has worked there for over 40 years and talks many languages send a post card home.  We decided to go to the War Remnants Museum 20k dong can't say it was a enjoyable time, in fact the total opposite, but it did help us to understand brutality and the horrors of war and the history and the reunification of Vietnam.  Had a drink at the bar on level 52 of the Bitexco financial tower and the view was FREE  For the real ale drinks I had a beer or two at the Pasteur street Brewing Company. If you sit in the front bar next to the window you can people (scooter) watch!

Could only see part of the outside due to extensive renovations. The area that could be seen from from the outside, it looks very impressive. Can only imagine what the inside would look like. Would be lovely to see when renovations are completed.

This Catholic church is situated in the heart of Hồ Chí Minh city, District 1. It's most famous landmark attracting not only Catholics but also tourists for its neo - Romanesque style architecture and sacred atmosphere. It is located near to famous restaurants
Unfortunately, the church was closed for restoration work. From the outside, it's a very beautiful and well-maintained building.

In June 2018, the church was under renovation and could not be visited inside. There were many tourists outside, so it is recommended to take photos next to the statue. The distance from the church is also good, making it a good spot for taking pictures.

Beautiful architecture. Cross the road for a better view in front and to get a good picture taken. It will be very hot and warm though so you must be patient to take your turn snapping a pic of the same from the best angle.

It is a place many people visit when traveling to Ho Chi Minh City, so it is worth a visit. However, it is not a place worth making a special trip out of your busy schedule; I recommend visiting it if you are already in the area.

You have to indicate that you want to attend Mass, then you will be allowed in before the barrier, even as a tourist. Candles are sold outside, at least during Mass (e.g., Sunday evening). Compared to European Catholic churches, the church is sparsely decorated, but it is frequented by more people and with greater devotion. Attending Mass was a beautiful experience.
Right beside the central post office. Beautiful building to see! They still hold mass, times were posted on the gate.

Unfortunately, we were in the middle of renovations. The entire cathedral was covered in scaffolding. The interior was not open to visitors. I haven't seen the original in France, so it's difficult to compare. Overall, it makes a very good impression, and knowing that every brick, ornament, stained glass, and most of the materials used in its construction came from France is admirable.

I went on a Sunday morning, and a mass was in progress, so I couldn't go inside. Those planning to go on a Sunday should be aware of this. The perimeter is surrounded by a wall due to construction, but I was able to see the front.

My visit to the Cathedral was very special and quite a lovely building from the exterior. Unfortunately, during my visit the Cathedral was under restoration and we could not enter nevertheless, I still found it amazing to view with the convenience of the Post office building directly across the street -still in remarkable condition. Don’t miss them.
The Notre Dame Cathedral in Ho Chi Minh City is easy to find; it's right by the roadside, next to the Ho Chi Minh Post Office. The entire cathedral looks particularly solemn and awe-inspiring.
From the time when France occupied Vietnam. The church is a good example of French cultural and religious influence in Vietnam.
Short visit inside and around this area. Easy to see this and the post office together in about 2 hours.  Ate lunch at Carls Jnr nearby and watched the tourist buses and locals go by.
Notre Dame in Saigon, it's a shame it's under construction and we couldn't visit it, but it's beautiful. It's next to the post office building, which is worth taking a look at.
It is just next to the post office. It was repairing at the outside when we visit, but it's still a place need to visit.
Just across the road from the Post Office, so two for the price of one, and they are both worth looking at.

Notre Dame Cathedral is one of 4 attractions that are within walking distance from each other. The others are the central post office, Independence Palace, and War Remnants Museum. This wooded area is arguably one of the best zones in HCMC.  We can't really compare this building with its namesake in Paris. But it's still a place worth visiting as it's one of well-preserved French-designed buildings in the city harkening to its colonial past.  It was unfortunate that it was undergoing repairs at the time of the visit. So we couldn't see the interior. Yet it tells us that Christianity is alive in a Communist state. In the end, it speaks of the state's religious tolerance.
Restoration work being done at the moment but can still see the outside of the building - worth a visit. Watch out for people trying to sell you things out the front - lots of sellers.
We visited last week and it was closed for renovation. We are not allowed to enter but can took some photos.
We saw this beautiful site yesterday, unfortunately it was closed for refurbishment so we couldn't go in but the site itself from the outside was still magnificent to see.
One should see it, with the Maria ala statue. In Vietnam, there are approximately 10% Catholics. Vietnam is more religiously tolerant than one might assume. Notre Dame is a testament to that.

Packed with tourists as expected but worth going to see even though it entry was closed due to renovation. The souvenir shops are packed but have some lovely though expensive items at the post office in the same square.
Amazing replica of Notra Dame. Currently being refurbished internally and externally. Still worth visiting
I wasn’t able to enter this church since we went there at night, but the exterior is really nice, very picture worthy.
When I visited in August 2018, it was under construction and I couldn't go inside. If you're planning to go specifically for this reason, I recommend checking beforehand.
Beautiful building with interesting history, but currently under renovation so only the outside could be visited. Worth stopping by, but don't set aside a lot of time right now.

Must have been built during the French occupation. It feels strange that the communist government allows the church to remain open. It's size, location and stature give prominence to the past colonial master as there are no other religious buildings that match it.

It is a place that everyone visiting Ho Chi Minh City makes sure to visit without fail. Situated in the heart of the city, it is a very convenient location to combine with other sightseeing. However, I believe that Korean tourists usually just take a quick photo and move on, rather than being deeply impressed. With plenty of shopping centers and restaurants nearby, everyone ends up visiting at least once.

It does look like Notre Dame in France but this looks newer and cheerful due to the colour of the bricks.  During my visit, it was under renovation so didn't managed to see the inside.  It in District 1 and a stone's throw away from Saigon Central Post Office. Take note that crossing the street to one building to another can be daunting but it's alright. It's a part of the experience.  Kudos to Vietnam for preserving the old building.

Built in the typical French neogothic colonial style. It is built with red bricks. It sounds a bit strange in the hot humid climate of Ho Chi Minh . It is currently under deep refurbishing and the visits are not allowed . It is possible to enter only to attend to the Holy Masses . The interior is simple with few decorations . A lot of electric old style fans provide a good ventilation to counter the hot and humid atmosphere. Is quite similar to Cathedral of Shanghai .
We went there evening so we didn't see much of the area. please avoid going there at night and maybe they can add lights to it so even at night tourist might able to see it.

No miracles witnessed when we visited (apparently the Virgin Mary statue at the front of the cathedral shed tears in 2005), however, its a wonderful looking building and if you happen to be in the area its worth a walk around it (the side without the scaffolding is best!).

Beautiful European architecture in the middle of bustling HCMC. Have been before and is essentially no different to most other cathedrals, however it is currently being renovated externally and you cannot enter as of September
The cathedral is under renovation, however it is still worth a trip to the neighborhood as it is adjacent to the Post Office and near the Opera and City Hall.
So it was under renovation so we just got to see the inside. If I remember correctly everything was imported from France.
French colonial building from outside, ideal for photos but really busy with tourists. Located next to the Post Office.
Beautiful building but closed for restoration at the time of our visit which was a shame as I would loved to have seen inside.
Not much to see except the exterior as the building is undergoing restorations works. Located next to the post office.

Username: Mr. Kim with the Ora disease
Comment: This is a place that every traveler to Ho Chi Minh City visits at least once. It is great for taking photos, and if the timing is right, you can even enter the interior. However, since this is where actual Mass is held by locals and Catholic residents, it is nice to look around inside as long as you observe basic etiquette. Just don't go with too high expectations... haha. (Personally, speaking purely from my own opinion, if I see Chinese tourist flags, I don't go. Even if I have the chance to visit again, I won't go.)

Unfortunately at the time of our visit the Cathedral was closed for refurbishment so we were unable to view the interior. However, from the outside some examples of beautiful architecture could be seen and it truly is a very impressive building.

Located next to the century-old post office, it's a very beautiful church. I've been here twice but haven't been able to go inside. This time, the exterior of the church was under renovation. If you walk straight behind the Red Church, you'll reach another beautiful attraction, the Pink Church.
The cathedral is currently covered in scaffolding. That is a pity. There is a magnificent square in front of the cathedral with a beautiful statue of Mary.

Was under restoration but there was enough pictures and writings to occupy your visit on the construction dividers. Beautiful photo opportunity and located opposite the post office so very convenient.

Because the cathedral was under renovation, scaffolding was erected on the side, and we couldn't tour the interior. However, it was a cathedral worth seeing even just from the front. I now have a reason to go again.
Look very beautiful, when I was there, the cathedral was under construction, so can't go inside to see around, just taking few photographs from outside
A beautiful work that evokes France, but I find very little resemblance to Notre Dame (Paris). It's a shame it's under renovation because we weren't able to visit it.

Like many Vietnam buildings, a legacy of colonialism but it is a beautiful gothic church all the same. It is more lovely on the outside as it is quite austere inside. It is currently closed but don't lament not being able to visit, you saw the best part. However, if you do want to go inside and are there on a Sunday it is open for Mass which you can attend. There is an english mass at 9.30am and of course many others on Sunday as well.

The cathedral is located within minutes of walking distance from Saigon Post Office. It is a very beautiful structure but unfortunately it is under restoration and maintenance and the heavy Scaffolding was erected to cover a large portion of the cathedral.
A very pink church, and something really different after seeing tons of temples. It was nice to walk around and just spend a few minutes taking pictures.

Very strange to see this colonial catholic edifice still going strong. I prefer a Buddhist Temple really, but the context and history make a photo worth it. Loads of Rolland Rats hanging out in the square in front add to the drama!
Currently under renovations for another year. Really interesting building with a great history. Bricks from both France & China

Interesting, a cathedral like that in a faraway land; it's a shame it's closed for restoration. In the chic neighborhood, there is also the beautiful old post office with overpriced souvenirs and cafes.
The church is beautiful and an impressive colonial-era building. Unfortunately, it is currently being renovated, so it is not possible to go inside.

Beautiful place to visit... Please bring umbrella for rainy and hot days as outside of te cathedral is kind f open area. Walking distance from post office n lot f cafe n restaurant around this place as well.
It was partially covered in scaffolding, but fortunately the front wasn't. Nice to visit. I don't know if you can normally go inside, but it was closed today. It is located close to the post office.

This is a lovely budilding located in the middle of HCM. Unfortunately, we couldn't go inside beacuse it was shut for repairs. If I were to revisit HCM I would most definately love to see the inside of this lovely cathredral.
However: construction work is underway this month of July. Masses are still being organized!
We only viewed it from the outside but it is an amazing building, they we doing renovations and had scaffolding all around it
This visit was before the main construction work began. The set featuring the Virgin Mary and the church makes for a picturesque scene. They were also doing wedding photography there.
The Notre Dame is beautiful and funny to see in the Asian context. It is currently under construction so it is not approachable from all sites and at all moments. Check that first.

Beautiful to see although it's under renovation and has some scaffolding at the side , it is a lovely building. Not allowed to go inside. I enjoyed hearing the evensong service . Pity that Diamond plaza is within camera shot , but that's progress I guess .

The cathedral is closed down for two years for construction but it’s definitely still worth a visit. It’s a spectacular building steeped in history from the French era. Our tour guide told us that the Vietnamese have invited the same French construction company that originally built the cathedral to carry out the renovations. It would be nice if there was a small area outside the cathedral where people could light a candle and discreetly pray if they wanted. I visited again at nighttime and it’s totally beautiful.
Great building, incredibly beautiful to look at, definitely recommend if you like old, venerable architecture, impressive and beautiful (I'm not religious)

Nice architecture and interesting to know all original material imported from France! Currently under construction and unable to go in when we visited but we appreciated from the outside. You'd only need a few mins and a quick photo before moving on.

Easy to get some good photos however some big wooden fences are surrounding the cathedral and it looks like it’s being renovated.  There were not too many tourists around when we visited early in the morning around 9am.
After reading reviews we through we would include the Cathedral on our self walking tour. Unfortunately some of the cathedral was wrapped for repairs but the main entrance was visible. Worth a visit.

It looks grand and magnificent from the outside with the red brick walls, the Gothic architectural style and Virgin Mary sculpture in front of the main church building. Unfortunately I couldn't enter the church building because at that time it was still under big scale renovation, so please check beforehand (whether the renovation is done ) to make sure if you really want to visit and come inside.
A pleasant place, a functioning cathedral. However, it's currently undergoing restoration. It's unclear when it will be possible to visit.
Nice big church, it’s more beautifull from The outside, The inside is dark. The area around The church is nice!

Currently, Notre Dame Cathedral is under renovation, so people can only admire it from afar and can't stand there to take pictures like before. Hopefully, the renovation will be completed soon so that young people will have more places to have fun.
The outside sure was pretty, but unfortunately, they were remodeling and no one was allowed inside. Not sure how long this will be going on for... Still cool to see the outside!
While at the moment the cathedral is closed to the public for renovations it's still worth seeing for some great photos. Across the road is the post office so 2 in 1 moment.

The construction is from the French colony so it hides so many stories about Saigon. The most interesting thing is that all the materials were brought from France to build this cathedral. You should have a small research about this place and trust me, you will be surprised for its interesting stories
Do stop and take photos. Well worth the effort. Go early before it’s too hot. Don’t get caught by men selling coconut drinks stay clear just say no.
It was great to see the area and we had some photographs as well. Local people were selling the tings to the tourists on the streets at low rates

Ho Chi Minh City's Notre Dame Cathedral, said to be modeled after the Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris, is a must-visit tourist attraction and a great spot for taking記念 photos. It is also frequently used by Vietnamese people as a location for outdoor wedding photoshoots. I visited the cathedral twice, and on both occasions, there was an outdoor photoshoot of a bride and groom.
Worth a look. In a nice area of HCMC. No charge on entry. You just need to be mindful of those in their for worship.

You can only see the outside. Scaffolding has been erected outside, so you can't see the sides. You can't go inside either. The statue of the Virgin Mary at the front is fine! There were people getting married and taking pictures.
We went here but the Notre Dame was under construction. Hopefully the construction wont last long. Lots of people.

It's a nice spot, located in the city center, and connected to many other attractions. There are many food vendors in the square, but there are designated opening hours, so it's recommended to check the hours beforehand if you want to go in!
It was a great building to see in the middle of the city. Has a different vibe than the other buildings in Ho Chi Minh. It is also in the middle of all the tourist stops, making it easy to check out!

Restoration ongoing at the time of our visit in April 2018. There are scaffoldings on each side so we didn’t get nearer. We did our picture-taking across the street to capture the whole structure. Appreciated its beauty from the outside, wish i got to see what’s inside though.  Be careful in crossing the streets in this area. motorbikes are too many to handle. Lol 😀

We went to the cathedral 06/11/2018 or for my non-American friends 11/06/2018. From my understanding the cathedral has been and will be under construction for a while, so we were unable to go inside. You will definitely want to check and see if the cathedral has finished construction before you plan to go. I still thought is was an amazing place to visit, but I’m kind of partial I just really love old churches. It is very pretty from the outside. And has beautiful architecture.

We attended of a night time, and were saddened to see that because of construction works there weren’t any lights on or anything, which made it a bit dull. Nevertheless, worth a visit to see the beautiful building!
Interesting blend of French and local influences.....plenty of coffee places always on hand, all serving a great brew.
Currently closed for renovation until 2020, however still a nice view right next to the post office so take a minute if you are in the area.

One of the most iconic landmarks for Saigon colonisation time yet still a beautiful church by itself. It's not big but the details are beautiful. Worth visiting, considering how well it is located in the city.
Amy and I visited this building which was impressive from the outside. Unfortunately there was a lot of scaffolding when we visited. We would definitely recommend a visit if you are passing bye.
The area around is large and clean, there were few tourists on a weekday, the back is ancient and beautiful, but we couldn’t get inside.
Th church is being renovates at the moment. It looks exactly like the Paris Of notre dame. It was fortunate that this Building was spared.

Although part is covered in scaffolding during our visit, it is well worth a look. A little bit of Europe in the heart of Saigon and across the road of the magnificent post office. When open, it has a service in English.  Both building are of a bygone French area and well seeing - they seem somehow out of place but beautiful.
The architecture and Color of this church was amazing. Sadly it was under renovation while we were in Saigon. Would have been nice to see in it’s full glory
There was a lot of work going on so could not go in. The outside is spectacular a lot of engraving. it was right next to the big post office so plenty to see
A lovely grand cathedral in the middle of ho chi Minh city. Make sure you take the time to visit this location as it is worth the effort and time spent.

We went and visited here and the front fortunately was not covered and we were able to get photographs in front of it. However the rest of the building was covered in scaffolding and we were unable to go inside.
The building is being restored as May, 2018. Can only take pictures from the outside. No entrance permited.
This old French cathedral is a historical building and easy to see right next to.the lovely old post office. It's easy to.imagine the former splendour but now the (re)construction is underway.

You will take many many pictures. It is so majestic against the blue sky. I did not get to go inside though.  Naeby is the Post Office. That means you cover 2 tourist destination which are within walking distance
Beautiful Catheral showing the architecture of the old city. Unfortunately the doors are closed and I was not anle to go inside.

It's under construction at the moment so we couldn't go inside. What a lot of people don't realise is that when you stand with your back towards the church.. you get a nice view of the building the CIA resided in during the war... most famous because from that rooftop, the last helicoptor saving people on April 30th' 1975 left...today, there is nothing indicating this historical place, so you need to know or a guide needs to point it out...

It's under large-scale renovation from mid-2017 to end-2019. It still attracts a lot of tourists both locals & foreigners as they hang around outside taking pictures and the nearby Central Post Office which is just across the road.
The facade is a very ornate mixture of brickwork and stone. It's quite a site, surrounded by modern buildings. Not sure when the restoration will be finished but not anytime soon.
Lovely and ancient look from outside, still there are many flowers and quite peaceful to have a pause during the summer

The church is one of the buildings left behind by the French during their occupation of Saigon. Masses are held in French in the mornings and sung in Vietnamese at other times. It is very well preserved and cannot be visited if Mass is in session.

Looked lovely from the outside, front doors were open so had a view of interior as cathedral was closed to public.. Relaxing area in front of building. Nice to see it and it is similar to Note Dame Paris.
This cathedral, built by the French between 1877 and 1880, is in a revised Romanesque style, blended with Gothic elements and based on the model of Notre Dame de Paris. A source of pride!
This beautiful cathedral is undergoing massive renovation work and we couldn`t go inside but it`s in a lovely area with a garden and other interesting buidings like the Post Office

Located near the central post office on the main street, this is one of the must-see attractions in Ho Chi Minh City. The street is bustling with people, and many are visiting and taking photos outside.

Great construction by the French. Unfortunately it is closed for renovations so can only see from the outside. In a city that doesn't have too many other historical buildings from the French times, this is a must see.
We were disappointed this beautiful building was being renovated when we were there. It was stunning even with the scaffolding surrounding it.

It is a landmark in Ho Chi Minh City that is worth visiting at least once, but it is not that impressive if you expect too much; it is decent enough for taking photos. It appears to be undergoing renovations right now.
As far as I know, there is no other ND besides in Paris. This church is unique in Saigon and the best place to take photo surrounding it.  Unfortunately, I am not able to see inside it

I only had to walk around the building because it was closed for renovations. From the outside, you can see that the left and right wings are being restored. The area in front of it, perfect for taking a few photos, isn't very large, so you won't spend much time here... 15 minutes tops.
A reminder of the colonial era. It certainly doesn't compare to Notre Dame in Paris. The interior is quite pretty, but not comparable to European churches.
A majestic cathedral located opposite the central post office and also not far from the botanical and zoological garden!

Beautiful building to visit in the city.  Currently undergoing maintenance so has scaffolding up, but this is currently only on the sides, not the front (mid-April 2018).  Can only go inside on Sundays for mass.
Currently closed for renovations. You can only view the church from the outside. It's very beautiful from the outside as well.
I haven't been inside but just outside. It's very pretty and a nice place to take a photo. It's on the route of an easy walking tour close to other attractions so worth visiting.
It's a simple church, but very Vietnamese. Why are churches standing tall in tropical countries so beautiful? It's also beautiful when lit up at night.
Short tour trip out side qith some historical information as closed for repairs but think it's open on Sundays for service.

It's a beautiful cathedral, but you won't be able to see or touch it up close because it's currently undergoing restoration. If you photograph it from a distance, the scaffolding will inevitably be in the frame. But it's still worth a visit, as it has a unique Vietnamese feel and is located right next to the main post office and the opera house. Take a stroll there; you won't regret it!

The Notre Dame Cathedral in Saigon was built by the French in the late 18th century. It is a historical landmark of Saigon with its ancient French architecture, and it has been maintained and restored by the French to this day.

I did a quick stopover to see this notre dame catherdal and it looks nice from the outside apperance however it would be nice to see the inside. Unfortunately it was not open when i visited. I just did a quick snap and go back to my base :)

The famous cathedral is located in district 1, in the very downtown just next to the Central Post Office. The building represents a typical French architectural style and is also called accordingly since the times of french colonization!
the church is still currently under reconstruction but it is a beautiful church. sadly could not get to go inside .

A stroll through the city's colonial zone wouldn't be complete without a visit to this Catholic church. The sheer size and beauty of the cathedral's rear can be admired and photographed from the nearby Diamond Plaza Shopping Center.
This place is beautiful with stunning architecture. The only problem was, it is having construction work on it and therefore was a lot of scaffolding erected.
The cathedral is currently undergoing renovations. We were unable to go inside. Nevertheless, it is very beautiful from the outside.

That is a nice cathedral to have a feel of what used to be Saigon in the old days. It comes with a typical Vietnamese surroundings that makes all the charm and interest. Too bad the city tends to be more and more modern...

This is a beautiful church located in the center of Ho Chi Minh City, not far from the central post office. I went there to take a quick souvenir photo. It's not an essential visit, but if you're in the area, go check it out.
Right in the heart of town it’s an easy see. If you have seen the one in Montreal or France it’s the same.

Classic European building in the heart of HCM city. The cathedral still maintain in good condition. Visitors are encourage to take photo from the outside and not inside the building to avoid disturbances to the holy place

The beautiful lines and the size of the structure opposite the historic post office makes it s must see but because of work being done this is not possible except from afar The nature oF the building makes it one that seek attention and the square sets off its beauty In a city overwhelmingly progressing Snd being reconstructed it’s renovation will make it a not to be missed location for all future tours
Still worth the photos & a look at the architecture. Take the time to add it on your list. Say a few prayers for peace & prosperity for the locals.

The cathedral is often closed. Sometimes it opens at 4:00 PM. In the meantime, one can meditate in the courtyard in front of the grotto, burn incense and pray quietly despite the noise of traffic, horns and dust.
As you can see from the pictures it is an architecturally nice church, so it is worthwile to pass it when you take a walk through the old central Saigon.
One of the old church in town but presently under renovation, couldn't go inside to witness how good.
It is located next to the Saigon Central Post Office and looks beautiful from the outside. We were able to take some nice photos with the Notre Dame Cathedral as background.
The cathedral is next to the central post office. Very nice church, typical cathedral. Nice church in central location.
Really enjoyed seeing this version of the cathedral. You can see the similarities but also the unique features which was lovely.
If you read my comment in March you will realize that the church was closed because of construction however it is the same church as in Paris so you can take the pictures

As a catholic, this was really part of my bucket list in Saigon. I have been to many historical and old churches but I cam say they did a good job in restoring the color of the church. Unfortunately, the chuch was under renovation last week during my visit. I still offered a prayer outside.

When, on a day tour of HCMC, we stopped by the Cathedral we were unfortunately told that the superb church was closed for repairs. So we could only admire its facade. We were also told that Saigon Notre Dame is now open just on Sundays for services in the mornings
Unfortunately, part of it is currently covered due to construction work, but it is a truly magnificent cathedral that is well worth a visit.
Beautiful place and very close to many other good attractions in Ho Chi Minh City. Worth the visit for sure.
It was under construction, so I only saw it from the outside. It's a very cute church, but since I only saw it briefly, that was it. The post office is across the street.
A beautiful cathedral, but currently closed to visitors as it is undergoing renovations and scaffolding adorns both exterior sides.
unfortunately at the time of our visit March 18 still being refurbished - But what you can see form the outside still makes this stand out as a lovely building
The cathedral is a great place to visit. This is a great architecturally significant building from the Old French Colonial era. The building gets beautifully lit up during dusk and evening.

Although one side of the cathedral seems to be under construction, the front part with the large Virgin Mary is very pretty. I would definitely recommend a stop by to look at the architecture of this cathedral

I was visiting Ho Chi Minh City for the first time in a while, and I really wanted to go, so I made a detour on my way back to the hotel after work. Unfortunately, it's currently under construction and scaffolding has been erected.

A very beautiful cathedral in a relatively quiet neighborhood next to the famous 1930s post office, still in operation... Toulouse, oh Toulouse (familiar tune), it's beautiful (alas for us, it's under renovation; this city suffers from its terrible traffic, but one can discover and appreciate beautiful Art Deco buildings there (particularly in the French Quarter)...

There are lots of people taking photos here—wedding photos, graduation photos, salon photos, you name it. It's right next to a bookstore and the century-old General Post Office, and there's also a McDonald's with a very French colonial style, so you'll see a lot of foreigners. Although it's under renovation, just be patient, considering its age! Legend has it that you'll see the Virgin Mary's tears on Christmas Eve; if you're lucky, come and try your luck... On Christmas Eve, you'll see a bunch of handsome men and beautiful women here...^Q^
Everything about Vietnam is interesting from walking the streets in the cities, crossing in traffic, learning the history, talking to people, shopping, eating. Great place to visit.
It's very beautiful, it's just a shame that the church is under construction and therefore we cannot visit it.

The church is currently under renovation and it’s not open for visitors. No visitors are allowed to go inside the church unless you are attending mass on Sunday. Only one English mass is avail on Sunday for Catholic. The church will then closed shortly after the mass is over. No visitors are allowed to enter.

Saigon Notre-Dame Cathedral is a defining French colonial landmark. Built from imported red bricks, its striking neo-Romanesque façade and twin bell towers dominate the city center. While the interior is currently under renovation, its majestic exterior remains a must-see and a popular photo spot, embodying the historical heart of Ho Chi Minh City. Too bad is under renovation and can view from outside. Also across is the Saigon Post Office.

I walk past this cathedral everyday on route to my favourite coffee shop from my tiny apartment in the Japan Town Red-light district that's perhaps half a mile away. The restoration has been ongoing since July 2017, and was originally slated for completion in 2020. Due to numerous factors, but primarily the extent of the damage, completion is now forecast for 2027. Though, I am not holding my breath, that that will be an accurate date.  Right now though, in the run up to Christmas, the cathedral is lit up at night, something that is a spectical in itself.

Don't get me wrong, I love this place—the architecture is stunning, and it's a true Saigon landmark. But come on, folks, can we speed things up a bit? I’m pretty sure I’ve seen glaciers move faster.
