After visit to Central Post office our guide tried to take us to Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral. Unfortunately, the guards said it was closed for renovations. From the outside it looks to be a beautiful Cathedral. Sorry we missed going inside
Although maintenance work is. Urgently underway, it’s worth visiting Bring your camera and enjoy your visit
The church has some lovely titles a the entrance. It is not possible to all diwn towards the alter. Worth a visit if you are in the area.

Besides the fact cathedral was under reconstruction I was a bit disappointed to find out that there was also some sort of giant flower covering the stained glass desings. If it was to protect the real thing I believe using plastic replica would have been more suitable. Pigeons were going about their usual business leaving droppings everywhere. I believe that they should install steel thorns to discourage birds from deteriorating the structures.

On February 20th, I went to the post office and bookstore street, only to find that the adjacent Notre Dame Cathedral was under renovation and therefore closed to visitors. It seems I'll have to go to Ho Chi Minh City again.
Has been under construction for several months, u can only take pictures outside, no way to get in.
Beautiful church, but visits are impossible due to ongoing renovations in the immediate vicinity of the post office.
This beautiful and historic cathedral is currently closed off with barracades during its reconstruction. Views from the street only.

Maybe it's because I'm used to seeing churches in Europe, but this one didn't impress me at all. No exciting architecture or decorations... Just a church. It also was under restoration when I visited.
This is a huge cathedral and its great to see that it is being restored, the whole roof is being repaired and its completely closed.
the cathedral is nothing really exiting, on top of this it was under maintenance therefore we could not enter and the outside was covered in part.

We would have loved to visit it, but it's closed for renovations. It's a beautiful cathedral from the outside, and since it's located right next to the post office and the post office café, it's easy to imagine that it was an important building in its time.
Perhaps the size of the building and its location are the main highlight of this building. Given that however more effort should be made to keep it in good condition for public access.

We went on 19th Feb 2018, it’s under maintenance and we couldn’t go in. The building is so beautiful that can take a photo outside. The central post office is just opposite. So, can arrange these two attractions together.
The neo-Romanesque Notre Dame (1890) with its two 40 m high church towers was only consecrated as a cathedral to Mary in 1959.
Currently closed to visitors but outside was nice to see. Very traditional like Notre Dam in Paris. In middle of a roundabout so look out for vehicles.
Under renovation but quite beautiful. It is not, however, as majestic as its larger French cousin.

This is a beautiful cathedral and it looks very contrasting amidst Asian architecture. You can currently only look at the outside as the church is under construction and will reopen earliest in two years‘ time (according to our travel guide). The cathedral is in a great spot close to many other museums as well as good cafes and bistros.

Typical boring european Cathedral. They are actually working on it. Restoration I guess. It will be nice for people who are into church architecture. Bllllaaahh. The Post Office next door is 20 times more interesting. 😂

One of Ho Chi Minh City's landmarks, located opposite the Central Post Office, the Notre Dame Cathedral is modeled after Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris. The cathedral isn't particularly grand in scale, and the interior is quite simple; it can't compare to the spectacular exteriors and exquisite interiors of many European churches. Personally, I think it's optional; Ho Chi Minh City doesn't have many attractions, so it's just something to look at.
Closed when we visited but dressed in lights for tet. Site close to the post office. Great bhan mi cafe nearby

I was very keen to visit the Notre Dame Cathedral during my visit to Saigon. After checking in to the nearby Oscar Saigon Hotel my wife and I walked up the short distance of about a kilometre from the hotel to the cathedral. However to my dismay, the cathedral was closed to visitors as there are extensive repairs being carried out. I had to be satisfied with a view of the cathedral from outside. I was told that it may be open to worshippers on Sunday 20th. Unfortunately it was not. The statue of Virgin Mary in the garden in front of the cathedral is blissful. It is said to have performed a miracle in shedding tears in 2005. People still come here in the hope of seeing this miracle all over again! I am told that this cathedral had been built by the French with all the material required having been brought from France. The outer bricks still maintain their original colour. The bell towers are tall and said to be about 58 metres. This is one of the few strongholds of Catholicism in a largely Buddhist Vietnam. I wish I could have ventured in. Hopefully I would visit Ho Chi Ming City Again! The road between the cathedral and the nearby old post office is a lively place. I found girls modelling on the road!! However though I would wish to give a higher rating, it would not be fair as I could not go inside. The three star rating is for the Virgin Mary statue!
It's covered in scaffolding and only visible from the outside. You inevitably pass by it since it's in the city center. It reminds me of Matseille, not Paris! A bit disappointing for us.

I would definitely recommend a quick visit to the plaza in front of this historic church in the heart of District 1. As with most Catholic churches in Vietnam, though, it is not open unless there is a mass going on. And then, it's a bit tricky to go in and take a look in terms of timing as you need to be either on the front or back end of the mass.
I have been to the real Notre Dame in Paris twice - looks very similar, but smaller. Only saw the front outside at night. Big sign - no visitation during the renovations.

Looks nice on the outside and it's just across the street form the post office and the park, so it's an all inclusive tour of the area. At the time we went it was going to some touch that may take a year or two but it worth to take a look since there's other things in the area.
There were quite a lot of tourists, and since fences were installed around the area, possibly due to construction, I could only view it from the front.
This church is located directly in front of the Central Post Office. Its French-European architectural style and historic building make it a prime tourist destination in Ho Chi Minh City.

We were only able to see its façade, as the cathedral is undergoing restoration that will last at least three years. We imagine it's beautiful inside as well, since there are photos along the barriers, but only of the exterior.
I’m sure the cathedral is great... I just wouldn’t know as it is under repairs. I paid to go on a tour and only got to see a statue out the front.

Unfortunately, it happened to be under construction the day I went. It would have been really cool if it weren't for the construction, so it was such a shame. I couldn't even see the interior, and since the entire side was blocked off by the construction, it was pointless to look.
It's a really nice building on the outside. Unfortunately it was under restoration when we visited, and you can't go inside unless there's a "special event". It was a bit disappointing.

In February 2018, tourists were not allowed inside because repair work was underway. Furthermore, it was said that only believers were allowed in during worship services, but ultimately, it turned out that only those known to the staff at the gate were allowed in.

Though currently closed for renovations - there are some good photo opportunities outside. The Virgin Mary Statue with the cathedral spires in the background - and all of the architectural features of the building itself.  This is located across from the post office - another must see. Don't miss it just because it is supposedly closed.
It was under construction and we couldn't go inside. We could only see it from the outside, so I thought it might have been better to just sightsee from the car rather than making the trip.

Third time is not the charm for me. Couldn’t visit the interior on 2 past trips, and the cathedral is currently closed for maintenance work. Beautiful architecture though, and still worth taking photos and viewing the exterior from the front and back. Best vantage point in my opinion is from an elevated position from the back (try the upper story of Diamond Plaza if possible).
A nice remnant of the French. The area has small shops and cafes and its atmosphere is especially nice. The post office is right across the street.

We stopped briefly at the cathedral to take a photo as there is a nice vantage point to capture the city as well as the Post Office. We stayed less than 20 minutes. There was scaffolding and construction all around which inhibited our view and movements.

Nice atop off but currently under renovation so no entry was possible (31.01.2018) also the post office is next building. Would not advise paying for a tour guide as you can very easily access these by walking or a small taxi ride.

One thing is so common in Vietnam that no guides will show you these French buildings willingly. Ofcourse can understand their views. They still feel they were being deprived from education by French people. This copy of Notradame is good but, poor copy of original ones. When we went there some major renovations going on so it was closed for tourists. From outside its magnificent.

The cathedral is under construction/renovation, so I couldn't go inside. From the outside, the cathedral is magnificent and well-maintained! Around it, there are cafes, the main post office, the book street... This neighborhood is quiet; it's nice to get away from the noisy streets for a bit!

As other posters have said, this cathedral is currently under renovation, so you can't get in it presently. This was our second visit to Ho Chi Minh City so we had been in the cathedral before, but would have gone in again if it had been open.
We were at the Cathedral yesterday, and the view wasn’t that great because the Cathedral was in renovation. So we could see the front of the Cathedral, but it was average.

A beautiful period building constructed in a style no longer used today (bricks made in Marseille during the colonial era). The building was closed for renovations at the time of our visit. Located opposite the main post office, it's a quick and ideal addition to a longer visit of the old town.
We had been told to visit the Church but all we could see was a large construction fence. You can take photos of the Church but it is mostly covered in Scaffolding.
Just a photo opportunity across the street since we cannot enter. You will pass by this along the way to central post office
Just another catholic churche
The church was under construction during our visit making some areas inaccessible. It's a beautiful structure and an important part of the history of Ho Chi Minh City.
An impressive cathedral but very disappointed we couldn't go inside after walking a good distance to get to it.
When we went in early January the Cathederal was closed, covered in scaffolding, but what could be seen was impressive.
This is worth the visit. If you are familiar with the history behind it, it’s unreal to see it up close, in person.

This time, I took a tour that went from Hanoi in the north to Hue and Hoi An in the central region, and finally to Ho Chi Minh City in the south, where I also visited Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral. The last time I went, I didn't go to the church directly, but I could see it from my hotel room. Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral is a magnificent church. However, it seemed to be under construction, as the church was surrounded by a fence and I couldn't see inside.
Stones built with soil from France. French colonials were digging coals and carry them to France and to avoid sea sickness they were making ships heavier with soil in their return to Vietnam
Could not go inside but a picture speaks 1000 words Definitely something to see when you go to Vietnam

Forms part of quite a nice site seeing walk around the city (we originated District 1 by the river) in which we covered the Post Office, Notre Dame (they’re side by side), the Ho Chi Minh square, Bidexo tower & food markets in a relatively leisurely 3.5-4 hours.  The cathedral is heavily scaffolded on both sides at the moment but is still worthwhile even if only to observe all the staged photo opportunities going on from weddings to the ubiquitous Instagram selfies.

It's a neo-Romanesque building built in the 19th century... Its patron saint is the Virgin Mary... It's located next to the Post Office... We were unlucky because it was closed for renovation, so I don't know what it looked like inside... But it's probably worth a visit...

A legacy of the French colonial era is the Saigon Cathedral, which even shares its name with the famous Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris. It was under renovation, so we couldn't visit the interior. From the outside, it could be any typical European religious building, but it seems out of place in Asia.
The architecture is beautiful. It's rare to see a church like this in Asia. It's currently under repair.

A red brick church towering against the blue sky. I couldn't go inside, so I only got to see the exterior... The weather was great, so the contrast between the blue and the brick color was fantastic. Definitely worth a visit.
The statue of Mary at the front was the perfect place to welcome visitors on a tour of Ho Chi Minh City. The church was very large, and its towering presence was magnificent.
limited access as being refurbished anyway but if service is in progress no access at all. does not have fixed timetable as is vietnam
If you came for that - sorry, but we´re closed. So make sure you have other options. I had a glance into it right before the renovation, so waiting for the reopening...

I couldn't go inside the church because it was under renovation. I only took pictures from the outside, but it was a magnificent and charming building made of red brick. It's located very close to the central post office, so you can visit both.

The cathedral was actually quite lovely to look at, but unfortunately it's currently being renovated and is surrounded by scaffolding. The building is beautiful from the outside, especially if you haven't yet had the chance to admire the original in Paris. A visit can easily be combined with a stop at the post office, as it's located opposite the cathedral.

Closed for renovations in early January so both sides were not that visible, only the front. Nice to look at, but certainly not spectacular as many others around the world. In a context of HCMC, with the few attractions it has, this was one of the better buildings though. We just walked past taking a couple of photos on way to Post Office across the road.

We couldn't go inside because they were having mass when we arrived, but I still think it was worth seeing. It's in a pretty central location, right next to the post office, so you can visit both buildings in just a few minutes and take some great photos.

Again unless you are an architecture buff no much more then a church to look at, they were doing renovations while we were there so couldn't go inside, not sure if you can normally go in.  Again probably part of any city tour next to the CPO. Cost nothing to look.

We did not go inside as it was closed for renovations. Our guide told us interesting information about the structure. We did walk around the church. We also went to the Saigon post office and got to meet the elderly gentleman who has been volunteering to write letters in English or translate letters for people who do not speak English. That building was very nice and the gentleman was amazing.
It's difficult to see much of this similar build to that in Paris due to the hoardings and significant scaffolding along the isle which has only been there 3 months so far!

An impressive building that unfortunately could only be seen from the outside during our visit. I don't know if this is always the case? The fact that there was also a renovation going on with scaffolding around the building also brings the rating down.
On January 6th, it was still under construction, making it impossible to enter, but it remains a beautiful building. It's located on the same square as the essential post office in Saigon.
A very beautiful cathedral near the main post office, but it was under renovation during our visit in January. A shame.
Sadly repair works are going on with scaffolding's around the building, could not even get a good picture, get a postcard from the Post Office over the road :-)

No doubt this is a great cathedral to visit however a word or caution. At this stage all you will see is the outside of the building and lots of scaffolding. Not worth taking a photo as scaffolding spoils it - buy a postcard instead. The Post Office is across the road and it has a spectacular hall and a couple of souvenir shops.

The building is currently under construction, so you cannot enter. I thought I'd take a picture of the exterior, but the right side of the building (the post office side) is completely blocked by construction scaffolding. Therefore, if you want to take a picture, I recommend standing a little further away and composing your shot from the entrance side of Dong Khoi Street, capturing the front of the building.
I only caught a glimpse of the exterior as it was under renovation. It was January, but there were still Christmas decorations up, including a display depicting the Nativity scene.
It's located right in the city center, and for me, viewing it from a distance was enough. Well, you can see it every day if you take a taxi for a city tour anyway...
Beautiful cathedral, but partly undergoing exterior renovation, which is a bit of a shame for photos!
Beautiful from the outside, but no access during repairs and no indication when it will reopen. Located in a central and beautiful part of town, so worth walking past.
The Cathedral was under renovation, not possible to visit inside. The renovation will og on untill 2020. Therefore not much to see.
The cathedral is under renovation during our visit on 1.1.2018.  Overall the architecture is still can be seen from outside.  Worth a visit
.... but only go there if you are really in the area. It's nothing special, also because (like in most Catholic churches in Vietnam) you can't get inside the church. Unfortunately

Another MUST SEE place in HCMC. However, the whole CATHEDRAL is under renovation, so tourists can only view from the outside. This is sad, as the interior holds the Beautiful Stained Glass Windows and other beautiful items with lots of historical value behind them. As usual.the place is jammed packed with tourists and their coaches, cars.....Visit it for the experience,and again for the interior when renovation completed !!!

Completed in 1880, this newer French colonial church in the middle of Saigon is completely different from the rest of the surrounding buildings on the outside. But it is a magnificent creation. Inside, the church is like any other similar church in Europe. You might even think it is Scandinavian, due to the whiteness of the ceilings and walls and the clarity of the rows of seats. The church is Roman Catholic and a popular place to visit.
Guards at the gate will not allow you in during services to see the building. That seems totally fair to the congregants but be prepared if you expect to get in otherwise.
We couldnt enter the cathedral because they are closing it for renovation. From the outside it looks historical, something European in SEA but it is just so-so.
You can not compare it with the actual Notre Dame. This is nothing similar. It is just the church in the middle of the Saigon, next to the post office.
Plenty of tourists outside as the cathedral was closed for major renovation. Interesting design from outside and its just opposite the Post Office. Located in the city centre.
Interesting to see this kind of architecture in a far eastern country. The building was having work done on it at the time of our visit..

I am absolutely sure that the church is beatiful. But it is covered by workers. I don’t kniw what they do. And also it’s closed. YOU CAN NOT GO INSIDE AT THE MOMENT!  No pictures, because it’s half covered.. 😢

Because I visited during the first year of a three-year recovery process, the entire structure was hidden by a covering, and I could only see it from the front. However, consideration had been given to visitors, and measures had been taken to minimize any impact.

You are unable to properly visit this church at the moment as it is closed for renovation works. No doubt in a couple months it will once again be an excellant place to visit, but don't go out of your way for the moment.
Covered in scaffolding so not looking it's best at the moment. We only saw it open on Christmas Eve for a service. It's in the middle of other sights so still worth a walk around the outside

Closed for renovation Outside is very nice. Next to the post office in the center of town. Maybe February they will open the church again. I heard you can still go to a 9:30 mass on sundays through a side door.

Closed, only Catholics are allowed in, and only in the evenings. You can take beautiful photos with the cathedral in the background. Half of the cathedral is under scaffolding, being prepared for reconstruction.

Immediately opposite the Post office in a busy put pleasant square, is the French colonial,Notre Dame Cathedral. The building looks like it could have been airlifted and transported into Saigon from any French town. Architecturally pleasing.
The cathedral apparently is getting a two year re fit. It’s a striking building in the centre of the city.  You can visit it and the post office easily together.

This Cathedral is one of the stops in the tour of Saigon, and is a very prominent attraction in French Square. It was built in 1880 by the French Colonists. The Bell Towers rise to a height of 197 feet, and the bells in it are still rung at certain times everyday. The brick facade is painted in shades of pink and beige on the outside, and the Cathedral is soothing on the inside with its wood accents, stained glass windows and simple statues. Unfortunately, we could not see too much of the back of the building, or the inside of the place, since everything is under construction and covered in scaffolding. In spite of it all, we could see how beautiful the place will be once it is completed.  Worth a visit just because it is historical. It is also one of the few remnants of Catholicism in a largely Buddhist city.

I couldn't visit the inside; the church was closed for repairs in November 2017. It has a little touch of France in Southeast Asia... (with the main post office right next door). I had a chat with an elderly Vietnamese man who spoke French on his scooter, in front of the post office café. If you see him, say hello from me.
This is a popular spot for tourists to take photos; it's beautiful. The sky was lovely when I went, and the weather wasn't too hot, making it comfortable to walk around and enjoy the view.

I visited in early December and the cathedral was still undergoing renovation work, meaning that it wasn't possible to go inside. The inside still looks impressive, but will probably be better once it's possible to see the whole thing.
Currently the church is under restoration. Many people are taking picture at the exterior of it. Given a chance should go in and visit the interior.

Very disappointing you couldn't see or go into Notre dame cathedral in HCMC yes buildings need renovation but at Xmas time when one wants to go to church and show loved ones a beautiful church that you had experienced before

Because it was under construction, we could only view the exterior. It was disappointing. The bricks, which were imported from France, are said to be still the same as they were back then, and the exterior was very beautiful, which was impressive. I want to go back and try again.

Still closed as it is under construction and a local suggested it will be for some time (>1 year). Apparently, it is open for mass on Sunday mornings, but I didn't confirm. Outside of the building is great so I imagine once it reopens, it will be a must-see. Located next to the post office so good to visit in combination

You can see it but you can’t go in it so if you like looking at buildings you’ll enjoy it if you don’t like looking at buildings don’t bother. Is it worth getting a guided tour for it absolutely not sorry but it is a pretty building with nice grounds.

The cathedral is being restored so could’nt get in. The trip was not wasted as the Post Office was across the street and that was worth seeing. If you’re desperate for a Maccas fix, there is a McDonalds next door.

This European-style building, reminiscent of the French colonial era, is a symbol of Ho Chi Minh City tourism. When I passed by in a taxi during the day, it was packed with people, but when I took a walk there at night, it was very quiet, and I was able to fully enjoy the atmosphere of Ho Chi Minh City.
Hoardings all around .... photo opportunities limited .... at this rate, the subway has a contender for first prize in the slow project category .. still pretty ... but ....

Notre Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon is one standing evidence of the French colonial influence on the city. Although it is a predominantly Buddhism country, some Catholic Churches are apparent and this by far is the largest and prominent.  There is an on going repair construction as of our visit this month (December) so we didn’t go in and look inside.  This is not located in an isolated area but right next door to the beautiful Post office which is also a tourist attraction. You might as well take a look in the inside of the post office too to hit two birds in one stone :) that is if you’re not in a guided city tour :)

Wonderful architecture . You can see how it mimics the Notre Dame in Paris . Sadly we could not go in as it is under renovation till 2018, we were told . Nice facade and grounds around for pictures . Close to the main post office .

This is built by the French and hence he architecture is influenced so.  I am told that the a wall on the outside is damaged and hence the cathedral is shut currently for visitors.  You can view it from the outside.

We went to visit the Notre Dame cathedral - the outside area is beautiful and when the sun is shining it takes such a beautiful picture.  Only downside - it was closed due to maintenance work and so we couldn’t enter the Cathedral to see inside which was such a shame.

If you have a young family, then Ho Chi Minh City is a dangerous place to visit because of the roads. Around the Cathedral and most roads it is not safe even walking on the sidewalks. Motor cyclists here are selfish and in order to beat the traffic congestion they ride their motorbikes down the sidewalks, plus in the wrong direction on one way streets. I was hit twice in one day.

It is currently under renovation but tourists can still take nice photos from the front of the church. The current Christmas decorations are very pretty. Good idea to include tjis if you are visiting the post office, independence palace and war remnant museum.
Incredibly impressive but most hidden by scaffolding at the moment and this detracts from its magnificence. If you want to go inside please make sure you are dressed modestly and have sleeves etc.
Touted as a replica of the Notre Dame in France, this one does seem like a poor cousin. It is a photo op of being in Saigon, but little else

We didn't get in the church, just saw it from the outside. The building and style are very nice, colonial style. However, in September they were doing some renovation works and we were not allowed to get close or get inside.
Nice building from the outside set in a large square, but has been closed for renovation for months. Now even the outside is a little obscured by scaffolding.
Beautiful from the outside, but due to renovations, it's impossible to visit the interior. However, you can easily take some lovely photos as souvenirs.

Being colonised in 19th centuries, this building truly show the legacy of French architecture rooting in this city. Looking at this building remind me of the Paris Notre Dame facade but with smaller size. This building can't be fully seen as some of it covered and closed for renovation.

Despite a mass being held, we were denied access to the cathedral. Construction barriers blocked all but the upper part of the building. A shame, especially since the work is likely to continue. Luckily, the charming post office is nearby.
The building looked great from the outside and I suspect it's equllly magnificent from inside but it is currently closed for refurbishment.  Can't really comment further....

As I am not Catholic, it did not hold much significance for me; however, as I passed by, the grandeur of the old, antique building was enough to warrant a single photo. Since it has been under continuous renovation for years, it is difficult to see the building in its perfect state.
Interior visits are only available at specific times and on specific days. The European-style architecture is impressive, which is one of the main reasons it is called the "Paris of Southeast Asia."
nice late xix church...but nothing special. Just walked by, is in front of the post office. It could be worht to read the short history of the building.

It's not far from the city center, within walking distance, and right next to the Central Post Office, making it easy to take photos of both locations. The building is beautiful, but it was closed the day I visited, so I couldn't go inside. If you're in Ho Chi Minh City, you must stop by and take pictures here.

Nice structure.. Under renovation so I could not enter.. The post office is next door.. Nice building.. Opera house one block away which is under renovation also. Next to the Cathedral there is a large park where you can relax from the endless traffic and heat.

Seen only from the outside, as the interior is undergoing restoration. Colonial-style, "modern" church, it's worth a quick glance because it's located in Saigon's central square, near the Central Post Office.
Cathedral was closed for construction when we were there, but structure from the outside looked beautiful, could only imagine what the inside looked like!
We were in Ho Chi Minh City at the end of November. Unfortunately, the cathedral was closed due to extensive renovations.

The Notre dame cathedral in Ho Chi Minh City is a nice cathedral, but for me, it lacks that bit of something that makes cathedrals in Europe great. It is worth a visit because of the aura that surrounded the whole place and peace it holds in a noisy city. I should say, that i didn't get a guided ture there and that could have perhaps made a lot of different.
Covered in scaffolding and closed to visitors at the time of writing (Dec 17). Worth walking past to or from the Post Office.
It's worth a visit, even if you don't stay there for long. Although I've seen so many churches, cathedrals and mosques that it's hard to judge. Give it a couple of years.

Well we did visti on Sunday 3 of December 2017 and it was under reconstruction and not only that because of the mass they did not want to let us in the whole day. So you do not see too much really. A big disappointment.
This was just a photo opportunity for me as my guide said we have no time tolook around but it is a good photo if you go early in the morning and avoid the crowds all josteling for a photo
Notre Dame is currently closed for renovations (hence only a satisfactory rating). Still worth a visit for a photo. The colonial post office is directly opposite.
If you are in the area it’s interesting to see a prime example of French colonial architecture at its best.
I couldn't go inside because it was under construction, but there are a few similar buildings in Korea as well. It is a good place to take photos.
It's located across the street from the post office. It seems to be under construction, so I couldn't go inside, but it's very Instagrammable.
Closed for major renovation, so could only be viewed from a distance. Never the less a fantastic facade seen on a beautiful sunny day
It is indeed a beautiful church from the outside, and its location looks great too. Unfortunately, there are fences around it. And it is closed. It is beautiful, though, that the fences light up. ;-)
If you are used to European Churches and Cathedrals then this is nice but won't impress you significantly. It was closed for renovation when we visited so can't comment on the inside.
Yes this is a good example of French architecture but not really what we came to Vietnam to see. Just another church as seen in Europe and transported to Asia.
Unfortunately the Notre Dame was under re-construction. So we had to guess how it looks inside. The outside looked nice.
The cathedral is very pretty from the outside. At least, it is between the scaffolding... Indeed, it's undergoing renovations for quite some time, so it's impossible to visit. What a shame...
During our stay in Saigon, the cathedral was unfortunately closed for renovations and couldn't be visited. However, it was perfectly fine for a nice "we saw it" photo.
Under refurbishment at the moment but beyond the boarding you can see the beauty of the French architecture. Good for a few snaps / selfies.
I could only see the outside because of the mass. Masses are held frequently in both Vietnamese and English, so it's necessary to check in advance.

Sides are covered in scaffolding. We got conflicting info whether it was open for Catholic Mass or not. Seems it was closed for tourists. There's a few interesting stories about it, but it wasn't a highlight of our walking tour.

We were lucky enough to find out as soon as we landed in HCMC that the Cathedral is closed for renovations so we didn’t include it in our schedule. We did however end up passing it a few times on our travels. There really isn’t much you can see at all with all the scaffolding and green mesh. Give it a miss and come back once it’s finished I guess !
As part of a sight seeing tour we stopped outside for photo but you can’t get inside and I believe it’s pretty bland inside anyway A lot of effort to ship all the parts to build it from France
The church is currently being renovated and unfortunately cannot be visited inside. However, photos can still be taken from the towers.
The cathedral is currently closed; visits are not possible due to renovations, but the very attractive Central Post Office is located right next door.
Unfortunately the Cathedral was closed for renovations and we cannot get in. However it’s worth the trip as we got nice picture of the Cathedral from the outside.

We so wanted to see this but unfortunately it was closed for essential repairs and will be so for some considerable time it would appear, beware that organised trips are still including it on their itinerary even though it is closed. So be sure to ask before booking.
It reminds me of seeing Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris. Although I couldn't go inside, I got the feeling that Ho Chi Minh was definitely influenced by France.
We were there around that area and saw this magnificent building. Sadly, it was undergoing major renovation. But worth watching the beautiful architecture.
Unfortunately, the cathedral is currently closed to visitors. It's surrounded by a fence. I don't know how long the renovations will take.

I stumbled upon it by chance while walking around the streets that day and was able to take a photo in front of it. Having been to Paris before, I didn't find it as impressive as I had expected. I visited this past July, but it was under construction, so I couldn't tour the interior and had no choice but to look around the perimeter. I recommend it if you haven't seen Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris! However, if you have already seen it... I think you might be better off visiting somewhere else.

Was told that it was a must visit place however it was under construction and there was alot of scaffolding about thus unable to see anything inside. Though it does look charming from the outside. Hope to visit it the next time
The outside is nice and is in the middle of a roundabout. Next to the post office. Nice to see and even though it is under renovation, I am still glad I went outside
The cathedral was closed in November 2017. Access is completely blocked. The building is beautiful, but for the moment...it's difficult to judge!
Beautiful looking building, unable to enter due to religious festival and unclear as to when next open when asked

This was on top of our list of must see sights but unfortunately it was closed to the public as extensive work is currently taking place on the exterior. What a shame we would have loved to see the inside!
Currently, it can only be viewed from the outside due to a reservation... and you can't see much from there. It's quite centrally located, so you'll probably pass by it automatically.
It was a small church, and there wasn't anything particularly remarkable about it, but there were an almost frightening number of pigeons swarming around a girl who was feeding them.

it is a nice looking church situated in a nice touristic area by the main old post office, but let's be honest here...this is no Notre Dame in Paris or Duomo in Milan, it is a tiny church that sort of looks like a cathedral. Nice coffee shops around, probably a good pitstop to send a post card home and relax a bit before heading to the next landmark in Ho Chi Minh.
Beautiful cathedral but unfortunately couldn't go in as it is under renovation. Right next to the Post Office.

The interior is closed on days when mass is being held. The stained glass is a must-see. There is a statue of the Virgin Mary in front of the building. The central post office is also right in front, so it's an area where tourists gather.

It's been a symbol of Old Saigon and new Ho Chi Minh City for decades.  Pretty interiors which are packed at almost every weekend mass!  Currently under renovation, thanks to the millions of Catholics who have attended service there!
Nice opportunity for a photo. 150 year old Catholic cathedral but by the French. Quite interesting to view. Not too much else to do.

We passed by Notre Dame, but it wasn't open to the public. We could see from the outside that they were holding a mass, but entry was restricted. I suppose it's worth seeing inside, so we'll have to go another time.
hen we got here we cannot enter as it was under renovation. Cathedral has good architecture and for Instagram posts.
Only seen from the outside due to restoration work. Still a lovely attraction. Outside, a splendid statue of the Madonna.

The exterior is relatively eye-catching, the interior is okay, nothing special. Perhaps it's because I lack religious beliefs, but some candlelight was restricted and photography was prohibited. The space open to visitors was also very limited. It's quite different from European churches. I'd suggest just taking photos from the outside. Of course, there's no entrance fee, so it doesn't really affect the experience.
Currently under construction, not much can be seen apart from large posters all around the square.

We couldn't go inside as it was closed off, but took several photos and had a short break. There're many tourists taking photos and resting as well. On weekends, you will see a young couple getting married and doing a photo shoot as well.
Unfortunately the Cathedral was closed due to a refurbishment so could only see the building from beyond scaffolding

One can only see the outside of the church and you may like to take a photograph here. It is opposite the post office and therefore suggest that you can stop for 10 minutes, take pictures of both places and head back into your vehicle

This beautiful cathedral in the centre of Saigon is unfortunately closed for renovation at the time of writing, however, it can still be admired from the outside. It occupies an iconic situation on a central avenue and creates a welcome sense of space in the centre of an extremely busy city.

The Cathedral is closed for 2 years for renovation, so you can only take photos outside. If given the chance to go back to Vietnam after 2 years, surely I will come back here.  Amazing Exterior Design, how much more on the inside.
Once you’re just a tourist you won’t squeeze much from it. Take a photo, enjoy, move on. Come inside once you’re pious person.

I’ve attempted to visit a few times, but it hasn’t seemed to be consistently open to the public. It was busy the one time I managed to enter. It’s a beautiful building to look at from the outside, and there are some nice stained glass windows that can be viewed from inside. Because it was so busy when I managed to enter, I found that it lacked the meditative atmosphere I appreciate when visiting a spiritual place.
disappointed we did not get to go inside as this old church is closed for renovation. It looks nice from the outside but the entire place including grounds are closed

I personally did not have a chance to go in, but i did get some nice pictures from the outside. I am Roman Catholic, but I really did not feel the need to go in. This trip was learning about our peoples religion and cultures.
We could not have a look inside the Cathedral so it was a bit disappointing. I didn’t know that it’s open only for the mass.
Nice/funny/striking to see such a recognizable French church in such a large Asian city. But, give me the Parisian version any day.

A must-see in Saigon is the Notre Dame Cathedral, built by the French. It's constructed in the Gothic style using red bricks. The pointed spires were added later and, in my opinion, don't quite fit in. It's currently closed for renovations. The post office right next door is more worthwhile.
We visited the outside as the church is closed for renovations. As in Hanoi it is strange to see a church in such an alien environment. Would love to have gone inside..
Worth a walk around quite an impressive structure and in fitting with the other French architecture around the city.
It's one of the city's most iconic buildings, but any European cathedral is far superior. There's an English mass on Sundays at 9:30 a.m., and it gets quite crowded.
Near the post office and Reunification Square - Exterior view only - Nothing else to do except take a photo
Great architect and surrounding , in the central of city area , you can even do walk tour with map to nearest places , like museum , shopping mall and central post office .

Cathedral located in the center of Ho Chi Minh City. Of great historical and cultural significance. Clearly decontextualized from its surrounding urban context. A Christian haven in this marvelous city.
Could only see the outside of Notre Dame Cathedral due to renovation being undertaken. Worth a vist.

Many people mistakenly believe the church next to the post office is the Pink Church, but the real Pink Church is actually a 20-minute walk from the city center. If I had to compare the two churches, this one is actually more elegant than the pink one. Of course, it's just a church, nothing special.
Unfortunately, during our stay, renovations were taking place and it was not possible to go inside, it is a miniature of the real Notre Dame.

It's right next to the Central Post Office, and it was very crowded. Unfortunately, we came at the wrong time; it was under maintenance, so we just took a quick photo from outside and left. It's hard to find a time when it's not crowded, so my friend really timed it perfectly.

It's not exactly what you expect to find in Ho Chi Minh City, perhaps that's why it doesn't dazzle. I could only see the outside, so it seems the opening hours are quite restricted. If I were in Europe (the church) I might have gone unnoticed.

The church is built of red stone. Opposite is the "Post Office," a building designed by Eiffel. The exterior is neo-Gothic, the interior is bare. Honestly, nothing special. It's impressive to find a Catholic church in the center of Ho Chi Minh City, where you can listen to Mass in Latin. The Team Leader

Beautiful to look at from the outside if one can see beyond the scaffolding. One cannot enter. I don't know how long renovation is gong to be. It is good to photograph and one can walk over the road to the old Post office. Hold one to your belongings ; cameras have been snatched from people walking across the road on the crossing.
Please note that visitors are not allowed to enter due to construction, except for Mass at 5 PM (?). It is a beautiful church, so it was a shame.

It was impossible not to encounter Notre Dame Cathedral when touring the primary attractions in central Ho Chi Minh City. Although a visually prominent structure from the exterior, the cathedral was not particularly stunning on the inside - simple and relatively plain. It was worth a visit to say that we had been there.

This is a very beautiful church with a rich history. Unfortunately, it was under renovation when I visited, so I couldn't go inside. However, just looking at the outside was enough to convey its solemnity.

This cathedral is beautiful but it is currently under renovation until the end of the year so we weren’t able to go inside. It is very beautiful but we only saw the outside. There is a man outside that paints the pigeons different colours.

They're worth visiting as part of the iconic complex: the Reunification Palace and the Post Office. They're important because you can see the French influence, but they're not particularly spectacular.
Unfortunately, the building was surrounded by a fence (perhaps due to internal construction?), so we couldn't go inside. The tall red brick building really stands out.
It's a beautiful cathedral, but we were disappointed to find that it's under restoration. The whole area is fenced up now.

The place is a replica of the notre dame Paris which clearly depicts that the French built thus to leave their mark in this region. It’s so similar that it’s not funny. A replica you can say . But people don’t speak french here neither do they learn it st school .
The church is beautiful but the whole area is under construction. You can´t go in to the chuch or even in the chuchyard but you can still get decent photos.
It's located next to the post office, with very restricted opening hours, which limits visits. Pay attention to the visiting hours.

The cathedral is closed for renovations at this time but it was interesting to see a cathedral in the middle of an Asian city that was completely imported. We could still take outside pictures just not go inside.
This copy of the original in France is beautiful and easy to navigate. In the evening, many people gathered around the cathedral and it was generally pleasant.

Unfortunately the builders are in so you can't see inside. There is a large statue of the Madonna outside. The cathedral is situated in a nice square opposite the post office. The twin towers resemble Notre Dame in Paris but that's where the similarities end - diferent architectural style, different size and I can't see my way to imagining Quasimodo swinging out of the towers. Worth a short visit along with the Post Office.
another spot that is advertised a lot but when you arrive there is not a lot to see or do. its close to the post office but I would not go out of my way to see either
Nice place for a quick stop but unfortunately closed to the public at the moment. Have a quick look then pop over to the old post office and buy a few stamps for the old fashioned cards.
Beautiful building but it's not open. Hoarded under construction. Can't go inside or even near the Church. What a pity !
The cathedral is right next to the post office. Unfortunately, you can't go inside, so you just stand in front of it with 100 other people taking selfies.
This beautiful church depicts some great architectural work, but it's a bit disappointing that it was not open to the public. Hopefully it's a temporary thing.
Good photos feom the outside while walking past. Very busy do don't expect to get a photo without people in it. We didn't go inside so can't comment about that.

Basically its a French built church slap bang in the middle of District 1 surrounded by busy traffic. The place was closed off for renovations & had large boards around the outside when we visited the area but to be honest I had no plans to go inside anyway, its just like any other old catholic church, unwelcoming & not very inspiring. Your time in Ho Chi Minh City is much better spent elswhere.

It's the most popular cathedral in ASEAN. With the ancient and exquisite architecture, it's the place that very crowded in the weekend. It's appropriate for taking a photo or prewedding photo. I think It's the most beautiful cathedral in southeast asia.
Unfortunately the Cathedral was down for extensive renovation works and we cannot attend mass. But we took many photos.

From the outside, it looks like a reproduction of the French original of the same name... It wouldn't be too bad, but, in the context of Saigon, both the interior and exterior have nothing to do with it, and in my opinion it's a bit kitsch, unlike the Post Office building, which stands on the same square and has a certain colonial character of its own. The only interesting thing are the votive offerings plastered on the walls.... There are worse things, though.
A great looking cathedral which I did not expect to see in Ho Chi Minh. You cannot go inside but it does make a very dramatic photo even from outside.
But we couldn't go in because it was closed. At the door is a statue of Saint Mary, to whom many Vietnamese women go to pray every night.
If you are into church architecture and you visit the Old Post Office then have a look at this old church for the architecture it is worth a look.
Beautiful cathedral, and we went inside briefly with our guide. A beautiful experience. We were there for about 20 minutes, and that was enough for me. Still, glad to have seen it.
The area around the Cathedral is always crowded with tourists. Worth a one-time visit to snap a few pictures and say you've been

It was beautiful, but I was sad because I couldn't go inside. I know it couldn't be helped, but I was still quite disappointed. However, I didn't lie and pretend to be Catholic; I just looked from a distance and left. I was scared I would be punished by heaven if I lied, so I turned back, but I still feel so regretful about it.

Not sure how long the renovations go for, so we could only walk past.  Church looked nice, if it is still being renovated there is a gorgeous restaurant around the corner called Propoganda, highly recommended.
Few minutes needed to take a few photos is all that's needed. It's situated right across from the post office
It's located at the end of Dong Khoi Street, next to the post office, so anyone visiting Ho Chi Minh City should definitely see it. I only saw the exterior, but it was beautiful.
It's not an incredible cathedral that you absolutely have to see. It was under renovation in September, and I only saw it briefly from the outside, but it's nothing extraordinary.

They sell you the Cathedral as if it were the same as the one in France, but it's quite poor and bland; it has nothing special about it. It's more of a curiosity because of its similarity to the one in France, but it's quite weak.
Beautiful structure, was under restoration so we only got to take pics outside. Plan to visit again when restoration is complete so i can get a look inside
We saw this building while we were on our way to the Post Office. Very beautiful and worth looking at if you're passing.

It's unfortunate that this building is under renovations at the moment as I would have loved to have seen inside. However, it is still a beautiful building from the outside and only a short stroll from the Independence Palace.
The catedra was closed and what we know it closed all the time. U can not even go near to have some moments in silence or take a photo.
Centrally located in down town Ho Chi Minh city it is an easy 10 min walk from famous Ben Thanh markets. The cathedral is beautiful

It was under renovation, so I could only look at it from the outside. There's a post office in a tourist area next door, and it's close to the Reunification Cathedral, so I think it's easily accessible.
It just a tourist spot which located just beside the central post office. Its worth to explore, but mostly we just took a picture and ran off since there are nothing much to see
We only saw it from the outside, and I found it rather uninteresting. It's interesting to learn about French heritage, but I couldn't find anything else to it.

Went past the Notre Dame however when I went they had constructed a wall around it and you weren't allowed to go inside or get close. Unsure if this is new or temporary, it is a beautiful building from the outside and right next to the central post office however not much more then that.
It's included in all the tours, but we didn't go inside. In fact, I don't know if you can even go inside because it was completely surrounded by fences. I didn't find it very interesting.
This is a cool old church, its similarities to its namesake are unknown to me, but it was a cool site to the in, in Saigon while exploring.

This cathedral is certainly not the top attraction in the city. It is quite beautiful, however it is currently under renovation so the inside is not open for people to view. Another downside is that is in the middle of a few very busy streets so it gets hard to go across to the center to get closer.
So disappointed we cant enter due to restoration. The building looks great and a lot of tourist hang around outside to take photos. Post office is just across the road.

Please hurry up and complete the renovations of this majestic building. I have been coming to HMC for over 15 years and it never seems to be ready to accept visitors. It is fine for a quick shot but you are better going across the road to the former post office and taking some great pics from inside.
Just a church which as a land mark in Hi Chi Minh . This is one of the hot spot in city tour list which can't escape.  Not much info and history to be explore.

It is a great spot for photos, but it was a shame that we couldn't see the interior because construction was underway as of September 3rd. You can hear the cathedral bells ringing around 4 PM, so it might be a good idea to time your visit accordingly.
There is nothing much to do when I visited. It was under construction. But from outside the structure is very good. Worst the visit for taking nice pics.
Entry is prohibited. Worth to go and admire the architectural work.  No ticket required.  The post office is just opposite, there's mcdonald with pork on the menu too.
The old cathedral is currently under restoration to bring it back to all its glory it a must visit to get the real history I'd suggest a guided tour of the city
The cathedral was closed when we visited on a Monday, so we only managed a photo from outside the premises.
If you come from Europe, this is nothing special, not worth a special visit in my mind. Worth a glance when you are passing by, I was much more impressed by the old post nearby.
When I went there, it was still under renovation, so I couldn't go inside the church. My family and I just took a photo outside and left. It was a perfect spot for taking pictures.

If you happen to be passing by and you're visiting the post office building, which is 100 meters away, it won't cost you anything to get in. Otherwise, it's very simple. It's not particularly interesting.

A must-see spot for tourists visiting Vietnam, this is where I always see local couples taking wedding photos. Whether you take close-up shots with the red brick walls as a backdrop or stand a little further away to capture the entire church, the results are superb. However, please note that the Notre Dame Cathedral is currently fenced off from the outside for interior renovations, which are expected to take two years. Locals say the church was nearing the point of collapse and should have been renovated long ago, but the budget and approval for the renovations were only recently obtained from France. Therefore, when taking photos here, you need to adjust your angle slightly to avoid including the fence in the frame and ruining the overall aesthetic.
nice design, histrocal place. In the center of the city. However, which i visit it, it is under renovation, only able to take photo on outside.
A European Gothic-style church. I've only seen it from the outside, so I can't really evaluate its interior.
It's a shame we couldn't go in because it was under construction... (ToT) But there are lots of other things to do, so I think you'll still have fun! The food is delicious! 😁
The cathedral is not currently open as it is having renovation work. Judging from the outside, this may be well overdue. It is opposite the Post Office.
It was under construction, so I could not go in the church. It is a big church in Asia. There is a famous post office nearby, so you can visit them easily.

It's been closed to visitors for two months and will remain so for some time. It's very beautiful from the outside. We'll see when it reopens. It's opposite the post office designed by Eiffel, which is well worth a visit.
i think abbys can be seen in many places in the world and it is just a quick stop if you want to, but give it a miss, it is under renovation anyway.. 1 year i think.
The cathedral, located next to the central post office built with the help of Gustave Effel, is currently closed for renovations.
The Vietnamese are immensely proud of their Notre Dame; the Europeans will say: it looks similar, but it doesn't compare to the Notre Dame in Paris. Nevertheless, it is nice to see.

Careful outside venue as pickpockerters are everywhere, as are vendors trying to seel there goods. It gets very crowded with tourists and all manner of visitors by the bus loads. Excellent location however.

Yeah this was closed for renovations so I can only comment on the outside and its too back cause I see other reviews of when it was actually open and you can go inside but there are a lot of people around this area and makes it hard to even get a picture from outside...

If you go straight down Saigon Street from the Mekong River, you'll find it at the end of the street. It's currently undergoing major renovations, so I couldn't go inside, but it was bigger than I imagined. The roads around the cathedral are like a roundabout, and there's a lot of car and motorcycle traffic, so be careful of the traffic.
The cathedral is majestic within a large outdoor area for people to take photos. We were disappointed as it was closed for restoration but still go and take you camera
This interesting relic from Saigon's French period is closed for restoration work. For the time being, only the exterior can be seen, behind hoardings.
This monument, in my opinion, is nothing more than an ordinary cathedral, given that in Spain we have much more beautiful cathedrals.
We did a walk around it and it is impressive architecturally as is the one in France. It is currently closed to renovation so could not get inside it.
A nice building from outside, but it's closed, you're not allowed to go inside. The square in front it's worth a visit, on the same square is located the Post Office.
Very disappointed, access is closed during the works, with no indication of how long they will last. The exterior is quite nice.
Unfortunately the cathedral was under renovation so we couldn't go inside. Outside is pretty impressive especially as it's surrounded by modern buildings

How can you rate something behind the fences surround by mcdonalds smelling the crap food full of tourists around in going into the post office , it's a great view from outside nice front gardens nothing to say just a brocken heart I have !!
It was under maintenance during our visitation but it was still crowded with tourist outside the church. Many coffee shop nearby .

An amazing cathedral which seems to tower over much of the Vietnamese buildings within its vicinity.  Right next door to the famous Post Office well worth the stop to grab a few pics. The cathedral was closed when we visited, I imagine it would be very impressive to walk through. Standing and watching the cathedral you could easily be mistaken in thinking you were anywhere in Europe.

Of course, this isn't even a replica—it's incomparable to the original in size, materials, or aesthetics. Nevertheless, the Gothic church's architecture has been faithfully preserved. It's always crowded with tourists, and a memorable photo with a Catholic church in the background is a rare thing in Southeast Asia!

The name Notre Dame sets a high bar of expectation. Very high. And it's not. When we went, they only let you in before a religious service started (we went on a Sunday). We went in 15 minutes before the service and were out 5 minutes later. From the outside, it's beautiful, especially when it's lit up at night. The inside isn't really worth it. Unless it's pouring rain outside, of course. Because then it's a fascinating place to visit.
It looks nice from the outside, but you can't access it because it's undergoing restoration for the next three years.

The building stands right next to the old post office on a nice square. Pleasantly busy, lots of traffic, plenty to see, including outdoor cafes. Unfortunately, the building is closed and cannot be visited. As a result, it looks a bit sad, with all kinds of barriers surrounding it. The windows appear to be boarded up from the inside.

You cant enter as it is closed for renovation. Only can take photos from outside. I would suggest that you dont include this venue in your tour. It is very close to city center. You can take Uber or grab or taxi and come to this place.  Once you are here (Notre Dame Cathedral), the Post Office which was built with French styles is just beside it. Dont waste money in city tours. come here yourself.

We were looking forward to checking out this old Cathedral but unfortunately it was closed. The building it self is quite grand but in need of repairs. From our understanding the Cathedral has been closed for some time and we could not find out for how long or if they are renovating or when it will be opened. If you are walking around the center of the city it is still worth a look.
It looks great from the outside but is getting run down and it is currently closed to the public and no idea when it will reopen as nothing appears to be happening

Couldn't get inside however it is still nice to see it from the outside. And the post Office is right next door. Watch out for the little rats darting out from the flowers as the locals encourage them by leaving out feed for them. Gave me a fright but after a while they are interesting to watch
It's an ordinary church. Also, please note that it is currently under renovation and we cannot access the interior.
A nice replica of the one in Paris... And that's it, because I couldn't get inside; it was locked. Is it worth it? You go, take a few photos, and that's it.
The cathedral was surrounded by fences as construction was going on. Hence, we couldn't go near the cathedral or inside of it...

A magnificent cathedral and a historical monument. Beautiful architecture that is very reminiscent of the ancient connection to France. Not a must but if you are nearby you should not miss seeing this building.
We visited this church on Jan 2017. However we only able walking around at the outside because the church is closed. This church is just beside the Central Post Office.

If you are European or Latin American this church will be just another church for you, but it is worth going because of the neighborhood, from the church you can walk a street full of bookstores and coffee stores books thematic, and from there you are also steps away from the post office that is a beautiful place to visit and sends postcards home !I recommend you to go.

We've only seen it from the outside, as the interior is currently under restoration and we've been told it will take about three months. The exterior isn't anything special, just another pretty church. What makes it interesting is the country it's in, which is predominantly Buddhist. In a small square in front of the entrance, there's a statue of the Virgin Mary. The post office is located on one side, which is another tourist attraction worth visiting.
There are fences installed around the church for some construction. You can not get inside or even outside close to a red brick wall. We don't know how long will take.

We were unable to go inside, not sure if its usually open to the public. A nice site, but didn't spend much time at all - took a few photos and left. i wouldn't rush back here in a hurry, but take a visit if you have nothing to do

Suggest a walk-by or drive by as there is scaffolding around it - it has been under repair for a year or more our guide told us. Can't go inside. Nowhere near as impressive as the original French version. There are far more impressive things in Saigon than Notre Dame.
There are specific times when you can go inside, so I recommend checking beforehand. I went without checking, so I only saw it from the outside.

This church is located in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City. I visited it along with the post office. It's a beautiful European-style church. Depending on the time of day, it seems you can go inside, but I was lucky to be able to go in at just the right time.

I was disappointed that such a tourist attraction was in such poor condition externally. It was a shame that I was not able to go when it was open (if it is open). Great place for locals to meet and a good point of reference when touring around.

All the action and activity happens on the outside. the surrounding garden/parks and nearby attractions along with great range of cafes make up for the heavily restricted access to the church. Only the immediate entrance way is open to the public alongside the proverbial incense burning and donation box. It's great architecture of course. Guides inside monitor the no-hats rules diligently. Take a minute or two on inside only, then enjoy the rest of the morning exploring the immediate surrounds where it's all happening.
While this is a pretty building, the day I visited the church was locked to the public... not sure why. Easy area to find with lots of things to do around here.

We went there today but just want to give a head's up...you cannot go inside!! Some sort of renovation is happening around the building. You can see it from the outside, it's a beautiful building but not super worth going to when you can't enter...just be aware!
Saigon's beautiful cathedral is made of bricks from... Marseille! Unfortunately, we couldn't enter the cathedral due to construction work.
A lovely looking building, don't get me wrong! BUT I went here to look about but the building was closed...unfortunately. Check the open hours (i didn't cause i am a mug).
Can only take photos of the church without going in. They only allow going inside for catholic mass. but even from outside, the building is surely instagrammable!

It was surrounded by a large construction fence, which wasn't ideal. But still quite nice to look at. We didn't go in; I don't think that was even possible. The combination with the post office made it interesting to see.

Interesting from a historical perspective but not much different from what one would expect of a royal palace. Bunker underground is nice. Would only recommend if you have enough time and nothing else to do.
Not much to say about this red brick church, which we didn't visit. The square was very crowded.
We arrived late in the afternoon, the doors were about to close, but we were still able to get in for a short visit. A very beautiful cathedral, well maintained.
Nice building but we did not get in, but, many nice thing to see and walk around the area such as central post office, book street, nice food

They're renovating the cathedral, and honestly, I thought it would be bigger and prettier on the outside. It's worth seeing if you're in the city center, but you won't even be able to go inside because they're renovating the interior.
Can still get pictures from outside, but unable to get in. Nice park at front, where there are often couples having "pre-wedding" photographs ( with wedding clothes on!)

A beautiful cathedral that solidifies Saigon's city presence.  Worth a visit as you will be in the area visiting all the other sites (Post Office, Independence Palace, the market etc.). It's en route so why not!
A really beautiful church both in daylight and lit up at night. It was disappointing, however, that it was closed to visitors on a normal Monday.

On my first day in Ho Chi Minh City, I went to see a church, but was told, "You can only go inside until 4 PM," and was not allowed in. I arrived at 4:05 PM. They were so strict that they wouldn't let me in even if I was five minutes late. I tried again the next day during a less crowded time, but only people attending services were allowed inside the church; general tourists could only go as far as the entrance. For someone like me who loves visiting churches, it was a little disappointing.
If you're expecting a change of scenery, look elsewhere. It's a French church, nothing more. Its only distinguishing feature is that it's located in Saigon ;)

Under the bright sun, the red-brick church looked even more majestic. Unfortunately, the timing wasn't right, so we couldn't go inside. We'll definitely come back next time. The square in front of the church is a great spot for taking photos, but please be careful not to trample the flowers and plants in the flowerbeds.

Another church that in Europe no one would be excited about and here because the French built it and it's big, everyone comes to see it. Next to it is the post office. For those who like it, there's a McDonald's and Dunkin' Donuts next door.

Next to the central post office area of ​​the Basilica of Our Lady, there is a church that some people call the Pink Church. I don't know if it is not open to the public or if it has limited opening hours. Those who are interested can check online first.
Lovely building with obvious french architecture. Not able to see much as not allowed past the main entrance area. Bit disappointing.

I visited this cathedral during a sightseeing stroll. It's located in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City. It's a popular spot, with large tour buses bringing in groups of tourists. If you have the time, I highly recommend visiting.
The Cathedral cannot be fully visited inside and there are restrictions for people wearing off-the-shoulder shirts (tank tops or BVDs).
The square where it's located is very pretty, with an old French colonial feel. The church itself isn't anything special, but it's active, and it's interesting to see Vietnamese Christians.

As this was next door to the post office we popped in, a large section is cordoned off for those who wish to worship and you have to respect those by being quiet and not snap them in their moment of pray although some visitors taking the ever constant selfie were a little disrespectful and annoying but this was the case in every other part of Vietnam. It is a French-style cathedral,

and I think that perhaps all cathedrals are like this.

They say it was completed in 1883 using materials imported from France, but it looks so ordinary that I wonder if it was really necessary to go to the trouble of importing materials.

I am not disregarding religion, but as a symbolic building...

It is said to be the largest cathedral in Ho Chi Minh City, built during the 19th-century French colonial era alongside the Central Post Office at the northern end of Dong Khoi Street.

However, regrettably, I just walked past the post office.

In Vietnam, where the majority are Catholic, you can often see people visiting the cathedral on weekdays, not just tourists.

The exterior is Neo-Romanesque style; honestly, I don't know much about it.

I just found that out through a search. ^&^

They say the interior is Gothic style, but entry is prohibited.

Unlike in France, two spires extend side by side from the ends of the faded red brick walls. On the way back, I looked at the back, but it was disappointing to see it roughly covered in blue construction tarpaulins.

The cathedral was under construction overall, so I couldn't get any good photos. There were bidets lined up like a beehive on both the left and right sides of the front.

My only concern is that they don't follow in the footsteps of France.

I heard that the cathedral's bricks and materials were directly imported from France and built exactly like the Notre Dame Cathedral there.

I looked this up, too.

Actually, saying it is exactly the same now feels a bit sad because we are recording the regret that the original burned down.

The guide told me that although it is smaller than the Notre Dame Cathedral in France, the architectural style and materials are identical down to the smallest detail.
Unfortunately, we could only see a part of it inside the factory.

This church, with these rough staff members at the gate, gives the impression that not everyone is welcome in the church, not everyone is welcome to celebrate the mass, and therefore not everyone is welcome with God.

Of the three cinemas in Tan Binh, Pandora City, and Galaxy Tan Binh, Lotte Cinema has the most comfortable seats. However, besides being far from where I live, I don't know about other customers, but when comparing screenings of the same movie at the same time, Lotte always has 2-3 times more empty seats than Galaxy and CGV.

Furthermore, the inability to book tickets via mobile browser is not very appealing to me. Not to mention, the current online ticket purchase account cannot change its password and only uses the basic password 123456, which is problematic.

The renovations are taking too long, and there are too many street vendors, sitting everywhere, which is very unsightly. I advise you not to eat the peanuts, rice crackers, etc., sold by the women carrying baskets. Sometimes they leave them on the ground, and if you're not paying attention, rats take advantage and sneak in. A few times when I came here to play Pokémon Gym, I witnessed firsthand greedy rats attacking and crawling into a woman's basket while she was delivering quail eggs to a nearby table. It was a terrifying feeling, but I bravely ran over to chase them away. However, these rats must be quite cunning; they ran a short distance away and then turned back to look at me defiantly.

The cinema is old but not terrible, however, the infrastructure, especially the restrooms, is very dilapidated and dirty...
Even as a man, I found it disgusting, I don't know what women think.
The ticket prices are similar to CGV, but I'd choose CGV for its healthier and more hygienic environment.

The cathedral is currently under reconstruction.
Still under renovation, otherwise a nice place
Only two stars because the cathedral is under renovation (January 2023) and therefore covered in scaffolding.
Unfortunately, it's under construction and closed to the public, so we can't see anything, which is a shame.
It is still under renovation and not open.

Under construction for over two years. Tourists are not allowed. Only practitioners are permitted entry, and latecomers are not allowed.
Currently of no interest.
It's a fairly large, ordinary church. Those interested might find it enjoyable. There were many people taking pictures.
The old seats were uncomfortable, but the staff were helpful.
It's been under renovation for months. It looks a lot like the current Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris, the one that burned down.
Renovated for years. No end in sight.
It was under renovation, so we couldn't go inside.
Under renovation. Cannot enter.
It's under renovation, so many things are not visible, which is a pity.
The toilets outside the theater were dirty and smelly. It was crowded.
It's currently under construction. There is a Central Post Office nearby, so you can go take a look there.
Under construction.
I didn't like the staff's attitude.
It looks much worse in reality than in the photos. Right now it's just a dirty construction site.
It's impossible to visit because it's undergoing renovations for another 2 years!!
Still under construction, impossible to visit
Unfortunately, it's currently covered in scaffolding and there's not much to see.
Nothing special, and it's currently under construction. A fairly conventional brick Catholic building.
It is currently under construction. I hope to see the cathedral become even more beautiful~
It is completely covered by the construction work
Too bad! It's under construction!
Everything's under construction, really; the only thing that resembles Notre Dame is that.
Currently a construction site, you can't get in.
Disappointing as it is currently under renovation.
Currently under construction. The facade is covered.
It cannot be seen due to extensive renovation work.
suitable for sightseeing
Lacking some features
Closed due to rain
Under construction.
As of October 2019, under construction
...has been a construction site for years and is therefore closed
Just take a picture and that's it. Can't go in.
It has the atmosphere of a historical site.
Center ten Mrt.
Under construction
Under renovation
Still under construction
Thank you for visiting and giving a positive review of Notre Dame Cathedral in Saigon.
construction zone
Thank you for visiting and giving a positive review of our place.
Not much to say really. Most of the cathedral was closed for renovations. Outside was very crowded but inside was closed off. For me it was a pointless trip there.

Internet page of the cathedral is unreliable, it says it is open from 8-11 am but unfortunately that is not true! The building is all around in the risers and is being renovated. Turns out it's closed for 3 years!

Been renovating this building for quite a considerable time now (years rather than months) and the only way inside is to join a service. With the exterior covered in scaffolding it really isn't worth the bother

It was one of the attractions in Ho Chi Minh City that we wanted to visit as the Cathedral is considered one of the most beautiful churches in Vietnam, unfortunately it was closed and completely covered with scaffolding, the only thing I could photograph is the side and the external nativity scene. The Basilica of Notre-Dame of Saigon was built by the French during the colonial period in the second half of the nineteenth century and has two very high bell towers of about 60 meters in neo-Romanesque style, with a beautiful square in front with the statue of the Virgin Mary. All the materials come from France , I could not appreciate the architectural details because covered with scaffolding , next to the POST is another attraction of the city, and in the surrounding area numerous souvenir stands .
